title: Supplication Before Sleeping
arabic: بِاسْمِكَ اللَّهُمَّ أَمُوْتُ وَأَحْيَا
latin: bismika allahumma amutu wa ahya
translation: In Your name, O Allah, I die and I live.
benefits: The Prophet, sallallahu 'alayhi wa sallam, always started and ended his day with dhikr.

title: Supplication Upon Waking Up
arabic: الحَمْدُ للهِ الَّذِي أَحْيَانَا بعْدَ مَا أماتَنَا وإِلَيْهِ النُّشُورُ
latin: alhamdulillahilladhi ahyana ba'da ma amatana wa ilayhin-nushur
translation: All praise is for Allah who gives us life after He has caused us to die, and to Him is the resurrection.
benefits: By reciting the supplication above, a servant begins his day by praising the name of Allah, the Giver of Life and the Bringer of Death.

title: Supplication Entering the Bathroom
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْخُبُثِ وَالْخَبَائِثِ
latin: allahumma inni a'udhu bika minal-khubuthi wal-khaba'ith
translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the evil and the wicked.
benefits: This supplication acts as a barrier between a servant's private parts and the jinn when entering the bathroom.

title: Supplication Leaving the Bathroom
arabic: غُفْرَانَكَ
latin: ghufranak
translation: I ask for Your forgiveness (O Allah).
benefits: The bathroom is one of the homes of the jinn and devils, so it is recommended to ask for forgiveness for any mistakes, intentional or unintentional, made while in the bathroom.
source: HR. Abu Dawud No. 30, at-Tirmidzi No. 7, Ibn Majah No. 300

title: Supplication Before Eating
arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ
latin: bismillah
translation: In the name of Allah.
benefits: The benefit of reciting Bismillah before eating is to prevent Satan from joining humans in eating.
source: HR. al-Bukhari No. 5376 and Muslim No. 2022

title: Supplication If Forgetting Bismillah at the Beginning of a Meal
arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ أَوَّلَهُ وَآخِرَهُ
latin: bismillahi awwalahu wa akhirahu
translation: In the name of Allah, at its beginning and its end.
benefits: Islam pays great attention to etiquette. If someone forgets to recite the supplication before eating at the beginning, it is still recommended to recite it in the middle, even if there is only a spoonful of rice left.

title: Supplication After Eating
arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِى أَطْعَمَنِى هَذَا وَرَزَقَنِيهِ مِنْ غَيْرِ حَوْلٍ مِنِّى وَلاَ قُوَّةٍ
latin: alhamdulillahilladhi at'amani hadha wa razaqanihi min ghayri hawlin minni wa la quwwah
translation: All praise is to Allah who has fed me this and provided for me without any power or strength from me.
benefits: The Messenger of Allah, sallallahu 'alayhi wa sallam, said that if someone recites this supplication, then his past sins will be forgiven.

title: Supplication Entering the Mosque
arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ افْتَحْ لِيْ أَبْوَابَ رَحْمَتِكَ
latin: allahummaftah li abwaba rahmatik
translation: O Allah, open the doors of Your mercy for me.
benefits: The mosque is the house of Allah; a servant should honor it and hope for Allah's mercy within it.

title: Supplication Leaving the Mosque
arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّيْ أَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ
latin: allahumma inni as'aluka min fadlik
translation: O Allah, I ask You for Your bounty.
benefits: Allah's earth is vast, and there are many ways to seek provision. A servant should ask for Allah's bounty to be facilitated in obtaining the provision that Allah has distributed to all His servants.

title: Supplication Before Performing Ablution
arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ
latin: bismillah
translation: In the name of Allah.
benefits: It is prescribed to begin every important matter by saying Bismillah, whether it be an act of worship or otherwise.

title: Supplication After Performing Ablution
arabic: أشْهَدُ أنْ لا إله إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لا شَرِيك لَهُ ، وأشْهَدُ أنَّ مُحَمَّداً عَبْدُهُ وَرَسُولُهُ ، اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْنِي مِنَ التَوَّابِينَ ، واجْعَلْني مِنَ المُتَطَهِّرِينَ ، سُبْحانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ وبِحَمْدِكَ ، أشْهَدُ أنْ لا إلهَ إِلاَّ أنْتَ ، أسْتَغْفِرُكَ وأتُوبُ إِلَيْكَ
latin: ashhadu alla ilaha illa allah wahdahu la sharika lahu, wa ashhadu anna muhammadan 'abduhu wa rasuluh. allahummaj'alni minat-tawwabina waj'alni minal-mutatahhirin. subhanakallahumma wa bihamdika ashhadu alla ilaha illa anta astaghfiruka wa atubu ilayk
translation: I bear witness that there is no deity except Allah alone, with no partner, and I bear witness that Muhammad is His servant and Messenger. O Allah, make me among those who repent and make me among those who purify themselves. Glory be to You, O Allah, and with Your praise, I bear witness that there is no deity except You. I seek Your forgiveness and repent to You.
benefits: Whoever recites this supplication, eight gates of Paradise will be opened for him, and he may enter from whichever gate he wishes.

title: Supplication for Breaking the Fast
arabic: ذَهَبَ الظَّمَأُ وابْتَلَّتِ الْعُرُوقُ وثَبَتَ اْلأَجْرُ إِنْ شَاءَاللهُ
latin: dhahabadh-dhama'u wabtallatil-'uruqu wa thabatal-ajru insha allah
translation: The thirst has gone, the veins are moistened, and the reward is confirmed, Insha Allah.
benefits: It is highly recommended for those who are fasting to increase their supplications before and after breaking the fast, because the supplication of a fasting person is one of the three supplications that are not rejected.
source: HR. Abu Daud, As-Sunan Al-Kubra Lil Baihaqi, Vol. 4, p. 239, al-Hakim in Mustadrak 'alas Shahihain No. 1484

title: Supplication Entering the House #1
arabic: اَلسَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ
latin: assalamu 'alaykum
translation: Peace be upon you.
benefits: The greeting is a supplication of hope, a hope that you will be safe from all kinds of sorrow and suffering, and be enveloped in mercy and blessings.
source: HR. at-Tirmidzi No. 2698, Abu Dawud No. 5094

title: Supplication Entering the House #2
arabic: اَلسَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَةُ اللهِ وَبَرَكَا تُهُ
latin: assalamu 'alaykum wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh
translation: Peace, mercy, and blessings of Allah be upon you.
benefits: The greeting is a supplication of hope, a hope that you will be safe from all kinds of sorrow and suffering, and be enveloped in mercy and blessings.
source: HR. at-Tirmidzi No. 2698, Abu Dawud No. 5094

title: Supplication Leaving the House
arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اللَّهِ، لَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ
latin: bismillah, tawakkaltu 'alallah, la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah
translation: In the name of Allah, I place my trust in Allah. There is no might nor power except with Allah.
benefits: Whoever recites this supplication, it will be said to him: "You will be guided, sufficed, and protected." Satan will then depart from him. Another satan will say: "How can you harm someone who has been guided, sufficed, and protected?!"

title: Supplication for Protection from Causing or Experiencing Harm Outside the House
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّى أَعُوذُ بِكَ أَنْ أَضِلَّ أَوْ أُضَلَّ أَوْ أَزِلَّ أَوْ أُزَلَّ أَوْ أَظْلِمَ أَوْ أُظْلَمَ أَوْ أَجْهَلَ أَوْ يُجْهَلَ عَلَىَّ
latin: allahumma inni a'udhu bika an adilla aw udalla, aw azilla aw uzalla, aw adhlima aw udhlama, aw ajhala aw yujhala 'alayya
translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You lest I should stray or be led astray, slip or be tripped, wrong or be wronged, or act foolishly or have foolishness done to me.
benefits: When a person leaves the house, they will encounter all kinds of things, both good and bad, especially harm caused by themselves. Therefore, it is commanded to seek refuge from oneself and from others.
source: HR. Abu Dawud, at-Tirmidzi, Ibn Majah and an-Nasa'i

title: Travel Supplication
arabic: الله أَكْبَرُ،الله أَكْبَرُ،الله أَكْبَرُ،سُبْحَانَ الَّذِى سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ وَإِنَّا إِلَى رَبِّنَا لَمُنْقَلِبُونَ اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّا نَسْأَلُكَ فِى سَفَرِنَا هَذَا الْبِرَّ وَالتَّقْوَى وَمِنَ الْعَمَلِ مَا تَرْضَى اللَّهُمَّ هَوِّنْ عَلَيْنَا سَفَرَنَا هَذَا وَاطْوِ عَنَّا بُعْدَهُ اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ الصَّاحِبُ فِى السَّفَرِ وَالْخَلِيفَةُ فِى الأَهْلِ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّى أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ وَعْثَاءِ السَّفَرِ وَكَآبَةِ الْمَنْظَرِ وَسُوءِ الْمُنْقَلَبِ فِى الْمَالِ وَالأَهْلِ
latin: allahu akbar, allahu akbar, allahu akbar. subhanalladhi sakhkhara lana hadha wa ma kunna lahu muqrinin wa inna ila rabbina lamunqalibun. allahumma inna nas'aluka fi safarina hadhal-birra wat-taqwa wa minal-'amali ma tarda. allahumma hawwin 'alayna safarana hadha watwi 'anna bu'dah. allahumma antas-sahibu fis-safar. wal-khalifatu fil-ahl. allahumma inni a'udhu bika min wa'tha'is-safar wa ka'abatil-mandhari wa su'il-munqalabi fil-mali wal-ahl
translation: Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest. Glory be to Him who has provided this for us, though we could never have had it by our own efforts. Surely to our Lord we are returning. O Allah, we ask You for righteousness and piety in this journey of ours, and for deeds that please You. O Allah, lighten for us the hardship of this journey and quickly bring us to our destination. O Allah, You are our Companion on the journey and the Guardian of our families. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the hardship of travel, from a bad return, and from seeing something unpleasant concerning our wealth, family, or children.
benefits: This supplication has two parts. The first is praise of Allah and the submission of the servant. The second is a request to Allah for ease in travel and in the affairs of what the servant has left at home.

title: Supplication of a Resident for Someone Who is Traveling
arabic: أَسْتَوْدِعُ اللَّهَ دِينَكَ وَأَمَانَتَكَ وَخَوَاتِيمَ عَمَلِكَ
latin: astawdi'ullaha dinaka, wa amanataka, wa khawatima 'amalik
translation: I entrust your religion, your trust, and the end of your deeds to Allah.
benefits: Three priorities of a servant that must be maintained everywhere: religion, trust, and deeds, because a person will be resurrected according to their deeds.

title: Supplication for Piety from a Resident to Someone Who is Traveling
arabic: زَوَّدَكَ اللَّهُ التَّقْوَى وَغَفَرَ ذَنْبَكَ  وَيَسَّرَ لَكَ الْخَيْرَ حَيْثُمَا كُنْتَ
latin: zawwadakallahut-taqwa, wa ghafara dhanbaka, wa yassara lakal-khayra haythuma kunta
translation: May Allah provide you with piety, forgive your sins, and ease goodness for you wherever you are.
benefits: Piety is the best provision that a servant must prepare for the Hereafter, because there is no salvation except through piety.

title: Supplication When Wearing New Clothes
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لَكَ الْحَمْدُ أَنْتَ كَسَوْتَنِيهِ أَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ خَيْرِهِ وَخَيْرِ مَا صُنِعَ لَهُ وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّهِ وَشَرِّ مَا صُنِعَ لَهُ
latin: allahumma lakal-hamdu anta kasawtanihi. as'aluka min khayrihi wa khayri ma suni'a lah, wa a'udhu bika min sharrihi wa sharri ma suni'a lah
translation: O Allah, to You is all praise. You have clothed me with this. I ask You for its goodness and the goodness for which it was made, and I seek refuge in You from its evil and the evil for which it was made.
benefits: By reciting this supplication, one asks that the clothes they have bought may provide goodness and also be a protection against various evils.
source: HR. Ahmad, Abu Dawud, at-Tirmidzi and Al-Hakim

title: Supplication When Wearing Clothes
arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِى كَسَانِى هَذَا الثَّوْبَ وَرَزَقَنِيهِ مِنْ غَيْرِ حَوْلٍ مِنِّى وَلاَ قُوَّةٍ
latin: alhamdulillahilladhi kasani hadha wa razaqanihi min ghayri hawlin minni wa la quwwah
translation: Praise be to Allah who has clothed me with this garment and provided it for me without any power or strength from me.
benefits: The virtue of reciting this supplication is that past sins will be forgiven, Insha Allah.
source: HR. all compilers of Sunan books, except Nasa'i

title: Supplication When Mounting a Vehicle
arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، الحَمْدُ للِه، سُبْحَانَ الَّذِى سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ وَإِنَّا إِلَى رَبِّنَا لَمُنْقَلِبُونَ، سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّى قَدْ ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِى فَاغْفِرْ لِى فَإِنَّهُ لاَ يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ
latin: bismillah, bismillah, bismillah, alhamdulillah, subhanalladhi sakhkhara lana hadha wa ma kunna lahu muqrinin. wa inna ila rabbina lamunqalibun. subhanaka inni qad dhalamtu nafsi, faghfir li fa innahu la yaghfirudh-dhunuba illa anta
translation: In the name of Allah, in the name of Allah, in the name of Allah. All praise is to Allah. Glory be to Him who has subjected this to us, and we could not have done so by ourselves. And indeed, to our Lord we will return. Glory be to You, indeed I have wronged myself, so forgive me, for none can forgive sins except You.
benefits: By reciting the supplication for mounting a vehicle, our journey will always be under the protection of Allah. In addition, we will also receive rewards, help, and a calmer heart.

title: Supplication When it Rains
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَيِّبًا نَافِعًا
latin: allahumma sayyiban nafi'a
translation: O Allah, make it a beneficial rain.
benefits: It is encouraged to pray when it rains so that goodness and blessings increase, as well as its benefits.

title: Supplication During Heavy Rain
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ حَوَالَيْنَا وَلاَ عَلَيْنَا ، اللَّهُمَّ عَلَى الآكَامِ وَالظِّرَابِ وَبُطُونِ الأَوْدِيَةِ وَمَنَابِتِ الشَّجَرِ
latin: allahumma hawalayna wa la 'alayna. allahumma 'alal-akami wadh-dhirabi, wa butunil-awdiyati, wa manabitish-shajar
translation: O Allah, (let the rain fall) around us, not on us. O Allah, (let it fall) on the hills, mountains, small mountains, valleys, and places where trees grow.
benefits: The supplication above is recited when the rain gets heavier or there is a concern that the rain will cause harm.

title: Supplication After it Rains
arabic: مُطِرْنا بفَضْلِ اللهِ ورَحْمَتِهِ
latin: mutirna bi fadlillahi wa rahmatih
translation: We have been given rain by the bounty and mercy of Allah.
benefits: By reciting this supplication, one believes that rain falls by the will of Allah (Allah's virtue), not by the stars, so they do not become disbelievers in Allah.
source: HR. al-Bukhari No. 846 and Muslim No. 71

title: Supplication During Strong Wind
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَهَا، وَأَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّهَا
latin: allahumma inni as'aluka khayraha wa a'udhu bika min sharriha
translation: O Allah, I ask You for the good of this wind, and I seek refuge in You from its evil.
benefits: Among the signs of Allah's power is a strong wind that can bring blessings and punishment, namely good and bad. Therefore, a servant should continue to ask for good and seek refuge from all harm.
source: HR. Abu Dawud and Ibn Majah

title: The Best Supplication for Seeking Forgiveness (Sayyid al-Istighfar)
arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّيْ ، لَا إِلٰـهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ خَلَقْتَنِيْ وَأَنَا عَبْدُكَ ، وَأَنَا عَلَى عَهْدِكَ وَوَعْدِكَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ ، أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا صَنَعْتُ ، أَبُوْءُ لَكَ بِنِعْمتِكَ عَلَيَّ ، وَأَبُوْءُ بِذَنْبِيْ فَاغْفِرْ لِيْ ، فَإِنَّهُ لَا يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ
latin: allahumma anta rabbi la ilaha illa anta khalaqtani wa ana 'abduka wa ana 'ala 'ahdika wa wa'dika mastata'tu a'udhu bika min sharri ma sana'tu abu'u laka bini'matika 'alayya wa abu'u bidhanbi faghfir li fa innahu la yaghfirudh-dhunuba illa anta
translation: O Allah, You are my Lord. There is no deity worthy of worship except You. You created me, and I am Your servant. I abide to Your covenant and promise as best I can. I seek refuge in You from the evil of what I have done. I acknowledge Your blessings upon me, and I acknowledge my sin. So forgive me, for none can forgive sins except You.
benefits: Whoever recites it with certainty in the evening, and then he dies, he will enter Paradise, and likewise if (recited) in the morning.
source: HR. al-Bukhari No. 6306, 6323, Ahmad IV/122-125, an-Nasa'i VIII/279-280

title: Supplication for Ease in All Matters
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لاَ سَهْلَ إِلاَّ مَا جَعَلْتَهُ سَهْلاً وَأَنْتَ تَجْعَلُ الحَزْنَ إِذَا شِئْتَ سَهْلاً
latin: allahumma la sahla illa ma ja'altahu sahla, wa anta taj'alul-hazna idha shi'ta sahla
translation: O Allah, there is no ease except in that which You make easy, and You make the difficult easy if You wish.
benefits: Ease comes only from Allah. Even something difficult can become easy Insha Allah.

title: Supplication When Afflicted by Calamity
arabic: إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ اللَّهُمَّ أْجُرْنِى فِى مُصِيبَتِى وَأَخْلِفْ لِى خَيْرًا مِنْهَا
latin: inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un. allahumma'jurni fi musibati wa akhlif li khayran minha
translation: Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return. O Allah, reward me for my affliction and replace it with something better.
benefits: A servant should recite the supplication above when afflicted by calamity with certainty and hope. Insha Allah, with this, they will receive a better replacement.

title: Supplication When in Debt
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اكْفِنِى بِحَلاَلِكَ عَنْ حَرَامِكَ وَأَغْنِنِى بِفَضْلِكَ عَمَّنْ سِوَاكَ
latin: allahummakfini bi halalika 'an haramik, wa aghnini bi fadlika 'amman siwak
translation: O Allah, suffice me with what is lawful and keep me away from what is unlawful, and enrich me with Your bounty so I do not depend on anyone besides You.
benefits: Lawful provision, even if it is little, is better than abundant unlawful wealth.

title: Supplication for Protection from Laziness, Sadness, and Debt
arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ إِنِّى أَعُوْذُبِكَ مِنَ الْهَمِّ وَالْحَزْنِ وَأَعُوْذُبِكَ مِنَ الْعَجْزِ وَالْكَسَلِ وَأَعُوْذُبِكَ مِنَ الْجُبْنِ وَالْبُخْلِ وَأَعُوْذُبِكَ مِنْ غَلَبَتِ الدَّيْنِ وَقَهْرِ الرجال
latin: allahumma inni a'udhu bika minal-hammi wal-hazan. wa a'udhu bika minal-'ajzi wal-kasal. wa a'udhu bika minal-jubni wal-bukhl. wa a'udhu bika min ghalabatid-dayni wa qahrir-rijal
translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from anxiety and grief, and I seek refuge in You from incapacity and laziness, and I seek refuge in You from cowardice and miserliness, and I seek refuge in You from being overpowered by debt and from being overcome by men.
benefits: Abu Umamah then narrated: After I practiced this supplication, Allah truly removed my anxiety and gave me the ability to repay my debt.

title: Supplication After Sneezing
arabic: الحَمْدُ للهِ
latin: alhamdulillah
translation: All praise is to Allah.
benefits: Indeed, Allah loves sneezing because it moves a person to worship. It is encouraged to respond to the praise of Allah by someone who praises Allah, and vice versa.

title: Supplication Upon Hearing Someone Sneeze
arabic: يَرْحَمُكَ اللهُ
latin: yarhamukallah
translation: May Allah have mercy on you.
benefits: Indeed, Allah loves sneezing because it moves a person to worship. It is encouraged to respond to the praise of Allah by someone who praises Allah, and vice versa.

title: Response to Someone Who Says 'Yarhamukallah' After You Sneeze
arabic: يَهْدِيكُمُ اللهُ وَيُصْلِحُ بَالَكُمْ
latin: yahdikumullah wa yuslihu balakum
translation: May Allah guide you and improve your condition.
benefits: Indeed, Allah loves sneezing because it moves a person to worship. It is encouraged to respond to the praise of Allah by someone who praises Allah, and vice versa.

title: Supplication for Good Character
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ كَمَا حَسَّنْتَ خَلْقِي فَحَسِّنْ خُلُقِي
latin: allahumma kama hassanta khalqi fahassin khuluqi
translation: O Allah, just as You have made my external form good, make my character good as well.
benefits: A charming appearance is not enough provision for a Muslim, but it must be accompanied by noble character. Therefore, a Muslim should constantly ask for their character to be improved and beautified.
source: HR. Ahmad, authenticated by al-Albani in al-Irwa', 1/115

title: Prayer for Protection from Satan
arabic: رَبِّ أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ هَمَزَاتِ الشَّيَاطِيْنِ وَأَعُوْذُ بِكَ رَبِّ أَنْ يَحْضُرُوْنِ
latin: rabbi a'udhu bika min hamazatish-shayatin wa a'udhu bika rabbi ay-yahdurun
translation: My Lord, I seek refuge in You from the whispers of Satan, and I seek refuge in You, my Lord, from their presence around me.
benefits: Indeed, Allah commands the Prophet Muhammad and all his followers to seek refuge from the whispers of Satan in all activities, especially when approaching death. Satan whispers evil into the hearts of humans, encouraging wrongdoing.

title: Prayer After the Adhan
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ هَذِهِ الدَّعْوَةِ التَّامَّةِ، وَالصَّلَاةِ الْقَائِمَةِ، آتِ مُحَمَّدًا الْوَسِيلَةَ وَالْفَضِيلَةَ، وَابْعَثْهُ مَقَامًا مَحْمُودًا الَّذِي وَعَدْتَهُ
latin: allahumma rabba hadhihid-da'watit-tammah, was-salatil-qa'imah, ati muhammadanil-wasilata wal-fadilah, wab'ath-hu maqaman mahmudanilladhi wa'adtah
translation: O Allah, Lord of this perfect call and the established prayer, grant Muhammad the wasilah (a high status in Paradise) and the excellence (above all creation), and raise him to the praiseworthy station that You have promised him.
benefits: This prayer is recommended by the Prophet Muhammad sallallahu 'alayhi wa sallam for those who hear the call to prayer. It has many benefits, including receiving intercession from the Prophet on the Day of Judgment, attaining the wasilah and honor in Paradise for the Prophet, and receiving great and commendable reward from Allah. It is also a way to show respect and admiration for Allah and His Messenger, expressing love and longing for the Prophet Muhammad, the role model for Muslims.
source: Narrated by Abu Dawud (529), at-Tirmidhi (211), an-Nasa'i (2:26), Ibn Majah (722)

title: Prayer for Forgiveness for Parents
arabic: رَبَّنَا ٱغْفِرْ لِى وَلِوَٰلِدَىَّ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلْحِسَابُ
latin: rabbanaghfir li wa liwalidayya wa lil-mu'minina yawma yaqumul-hisab
translation: Our Lord, forgive me and my parents and all the believers on the Day the account is established.
benefits: This is a prayer of Prophet Ibrahim (peace be upon him), teaching us to honor and love our parents, remember the Day of Judgment, and care for fellow believers. By making this prayer, we hope that Allah will forgive our sins and make us among the faithful.

title: Prayer for Goodness in Both Worlds
arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
latin: rabbana atina fid-dunya hasanatan wa fil-akhirati hasanatan wa qina 'adhaban-nar
translation: Our Lord, grant us goodness in this world and goodness in the Hereafter, and protect us from the torment of the Fire.
benefits: This is one of the most beloved duas in Islam, combining all good of this life and the Hereafter in a single supplication. The Prophet ﷺ used to recite it frequently, and Allah mentions it as the prayer of those who reflect deeply.

title: Prayer for Patience and Victory
arabic: رَبَّنَا أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَانصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
latin: rabbana afrigh 'alayna sabran wa thabbit aqdamana wansurna 'alal-qawmil kafirin
translation: Our Lord, pour patience upon us, make our steps firm, and grant us victory over those who reject faith.
benefits: This was the prayer of Talut (Saul) and the believers when they faced Goliath. Despite being vastly outnumbered, those who prayed this dua and remained steadfast were granted victory by Allah.

title: Last Two Verses of Surah Al-Baqarah
arabic: آمَنَ الرَّسُولُ بِمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِن رَّبِّهِ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَمَلَائِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّن رُّسُلِهِ وَقَالُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ ﴿٢٨٥﴾ لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَعَلَيْهَا مَا اكْتَسَبَتْ رَبَّنَا لَا تُؤَاخِذْنَا إِن نَّسِينَا أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا رَبَّنَا وَلَا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَا إِصْرًا كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِنَا رَبَّنَا وَلَا تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِ وَاعْفُ عَنَّا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَا أَنتَ مَوْلَانَا فَانصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ ﴿٢٨٦﴾
latin: amanar-rasulu bima unzila ilayhi mir-rabbihi wal-mu'minun, kullun amana billahi wa mala'ikatihi wa kutubihi wa rusulihi la nufarriqu bayna ahadin min rusulih, wa qalu sami'na wa ata'na, ghufranaka rabbana wa ilaykil-masir. la yukallifullahu nafsan illa wus'aha, laha ma kasabat wa 'alayha mak-tasabat, rabbana la tu'akhidhna in nasina aw akhta'na, rabbana wa la tahmil 'alayna isran kama hamaltahu 'alal-ladhina min qablina, rabbana wa la tuhammilna ma la taqata lana bih, wa'fu 'anna waghfir lana warhamna anta mawlana fansurna 'alal-qawmil-kafirin
translation: The Messenger has believed in what was revealed to him from his Lord, and so have the believers. All of them have believed in Allah, His angels, His books, and His messengers, saying: 'We make no distinction between any of His messengers.' And they say: 'We hear and we obey. Forgive us, our Lord, and to You is the final return.' (285) Allah does not burden a soul beyond that it can bear. It will have what it has earned, and upon it what it has incurred. Our Lord, do not impose blame upon us if we have forgotten or made an error. Our Lord, do not lay upon us a burden like that which You laid upon those before us. Our Lord, do not burden us with what we have no ability to bear. And pardon us, forgive us, and have mercy upon us. You are our Protector, so give us victory over the disbelieving people. (286)
benefits: These are the last two verses of Surah Al-Baqarah, gifted to the Prophet ﷺ on the night of Mi'raj. He said: 'Whoever recites the last two verses of Surah Al-Baqarah at night, they will suffice him.' (Bukhari & Muslim). Verse 285 is the perfect affirmation of faith (iman), while verse 286 contains three beautiful supplications: for ease of burden, for forgiveness of errors, and for mercy and victory.

title: Prayer for Steadfast Hearts
arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنَا وَهَبْ لَنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ رَحْمَةً إِنَّكَ أَنتَ الْوَهَّابُ
latin: rabbana la tuzigh qulobana ba'da idh hadaytana wahab lana min ladunka rahmatan innaka antal-wahhab
translation: Our Lord, do not let our hearts deviate after You have guided us. Grant us mercy from Yourself; truly, You are the Bestower.
benefits: This is the prayer of the people of firm knowledge (ulul albab). They recognize the danger of the heart straying after guidance, and humbly ask Allah to preserve them in faith. The Prophet ﷺ used to say this dua frequently.

title: Acknowledgment of the Day of Gathering
arabic: رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ جَامِعُ النَّاسِ لِيَوْمٍ لَّا رَيْبَ فِيهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُخْلِفُ الْمِيعَادَ
latin: rabbana innaka jami'un-nas li-yawmin la rayba fihi innallaha la yukhlifu al-mi'ad
translation: Our Lord, You will surely gather all people for a Day about which there is no doubt. Truly, Allah never breaks His promise.
benefits: Reciting this prayer affirms our certainty in the Day of Resurrection and Allah's unfailing promise. It is a reminder that every soul will be held accountable, which motivates righteous action.

title: Prayer of the Believers for Forgiveness from Hellfire
arabic: رَبَّنَا إِنَّنَا آمَنَّا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
latin: rabbana innana amanna faghfir lana dhunubana wa qina 'adhaban-nar
translation: Our Lord, we have believed, so forgive our sins and save us from the torment of the Fire.
benefits: This is the prayer of the patient, the truthful, the devout, the charitable, and those who seek forgiveness before dawn — the qualities Allah praises in the preceding verses. They affirm faith and immediately ask for forgiveness and protection.

title: Prayer to Be Counted Among the Witnesses
arabic: رَبَّنَا آمَنَّا بِمَا أَنزَلْتَ وَاتَّبَعْنَا الرَّسُولَ فَاكْتُبْنَا مَعَ الشَّاهِدِينَ
latin: rabbana amanna bima anzalta wattaba'nar-rasula fak-tubna ma'ash-shahidin
translation: Our Lord, we believe in what You have revealed and we follow the Messenger, so count us among the witnesses.
benefits: This is the prayer of the disciples (hawariyyun) of Prophet Isa (Jesus, peace be upon him). It is a beautiful dua for every believer — to declare faith in divine revelation, to follow the Prophet, and to ask Allah to record us among the witnesses to the truth.

title: Prayer for Forgiveness, Steadfastness, and Victory
arabic: رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَإِسْرَافَنَا فِي أَمْرِنَا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَانصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
latin: rabbana ighfir lana dhunubana wa israfana fi amrina wa thabbit aqdamana wansurna 'alal-qawmil kafirin
translation: Our Lord, forgive our sins and our excesses in our affairs, make our feet firm, and grant us victory over the disbelieving people.
benefits: This was the prayer of prophets and their followers in the face of adversity. They acknowledged their own shortcomings before asking for victory — a powerful lesson in humility. Allah answered with the promise: 'So Allah gave them the reward of this world and the finest reward of the Hereafter.'

title: Prayer of Those Who Reflect on Creation
arabic: رَبَّنَا مَا خَلَقْتَ هَذَا بَاطِلًا سُبْحَانَكَ فَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
latin: rabbana ma khalaqta hadha batilan subhanaka faqina 'adhaban-nar
translation: Our Lord, You have not created this in vain. Glory be to You! So protect us from the punishment of the Fire.
benefits: This is the prayer of the people of deep understanding (ulul albab) who reflect on the creation of the heavens and the earth. Their reflection leads them to affirm Allah's glory and urgently ask for protection from the Fire.

title: Prayer of the Believer Who Answered the Call to Faith
arabic: رَبَّنَا إِنَّنَا سَمِعْنَا مُنَادِيًا يُنَادِي لِلْإِيمَانِ أَنْ آمِنُوا بِرَبِّكُمْ فَآمَنَّا رَبَّنَا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَكَفِّرْ عَنَّا سَيِّئَاتِنَا وَتَوَفَّنَا مَعَ الْأَبْرَارِ رَبَّنَا وَآتِنَا مَا وَعَدتَّنَا عَلَى رُسُلِكَ وَلَا تُخْزِنَا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ إِنَّكَ لَا تُخْلِفُ الْمِيعَادَ
latin: rabbana innana sami'na munadiyan yunadi lil-imani an aminu bi-rabbikum fa-amanna, rabbana faghfir lana dhunubana wa kaffir 'anna sayyi'atina wa tawaffana ma'al-abrar, rabbana wa atina ma wa'adtana 'ala rusulika wa la tukhzina yawmal-qiyamah innaka la tukhlifu al-mi'ad
translation: Our Lord, we heard a caller summoning to faith: 'Believe in your Lord,' and we believed. Our Lord, forgive our sins, wipe away our misdeeds, and let us die among the righteous. Our Lord, give us what You promised through Your messengers, and do not disgrace us on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed You never break Your promise.
benefits: This is a profound and comprehensive dua covering three vital requests: forgiveness and a righteous death, the fulfillment of Allah's promise, and protection from disgrace on the Day of Judgment. It is the prayer of those who wholeheartedly answered the call of faith.

title: Prayer of Adam and Eve: Repentance and Mercy
arabic: رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَا أَنفُسَنَا وَإِن لَّمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
latin: rabbana dhalamna anfusana wa in lam taghfir lana wa tarhamna lanakununna minal-khashirin
translation: Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves, and if You do not forgive us and have mercy on us, we will surely be among the losers.
benefits: This is the sincere repentance of Prophet Adam and Hawwa (Eve) after they were expelled from Paradise. It is a perfect model of tawbah — acknowledging wrongdoing, recognizing total dependence on Allah's mercy, and expressing no excuses. Allah accepted their repentance.

title: Prayer Not to Be Among the Wrongdoers
arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تَجْعَلْنَا مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ
latin: rabbana la taj'alna ma'al-qawmiz-dhalimin
translation: Our Lord, do not place us among the wrongdoing people.
benefits: This is the urgent prayer of those on the A'raf (the elevated barrier between Paradise and Hell) on the Day of Judgment. Upon seeing the Fire and its people, they desperately ask Allah not to be placed among the wrongdoers — a reminder to continuously seek protection from injustice and its consequences.

title: Prayer of Prophet Shu'ayb for Just Judgment
arabic: رَبَّنَا افْتَحْ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَ قَوْمِنَا بِالْحَقِّ وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ الْفَاتِحِينَ
latin: rabbana iftah baynana wa bayna qawmina bil-haqq wa anta khayrul-fatihin
translation: Our Lord, judge between us and our people with the truth, for You are the best of judges.
benefits: This is the prayer of Prophet Shu'ayb (peace be upon him) when his people threatened him and his followers. He surrendered the matter entirely to Allah, trusting in His perfect justice. Recite this when seeking divine resolution in disputes.

title: Prayer for Protection from Oppressors
arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تَجْعَلْنَا فِتْنَةً لِّلْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ وَنَجِّنَا بِرَحْمَتِكَ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
latin: rabbana la taj'alna fitnatan lil-qawmid-dhalimin wa najjina bi-rahmatika minal-qawmil kafirin
translation: Our Lord, do not make us a trial for the wrongdoing people, and rescue us by Your mercy from those who deny the truth.
benefits: This is the prayer of the believers who stood with Musa (Moses) and placed their trust in Allah against Pharaoh's oppression. They asked not to be used as a means of trial for the oppressors, and sought deliverance through Allah's mercy alone.

title: Prayer Acknowledging Allah's Complete Knowledge
arabic: رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ تَعْلَمُ مَا نُخْفِي وَمَا نُعْلِنُ وَمَا يَخْفَى عَلَى اللَّهِ مِن شَيْءٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ
latin: rabbana innaka ta'lamu ma nukhfi wa ma nu'linu wa ma yakhfa 'alallahi min shay'in fil-ardi wa la fis-sama'
translation: Our Lord, You truly know what we conceal and what we reveal. Nothing in the earth or the heavens is hidden from Allah.
benefits: Part of Prophet Ibrahim's (peace be upon him) prayer. It is an expression of complete transparency before Allah — acknowledging that He knows our innermost secrets. Reciting this cultivates sincerity and deep awareness of Allah (taqwa).

title: Prayer for Establishing Regular Prayer
arabic: رَبِّ اجْعَلْنِي مُقِيمَ الصَّلَاةِ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِي رَبَّنَا وَتَقَبَّلْ دُعَاءِ
latin: rabbij'alni muqimas-salati wa min dhurriyyati rabbana wa taqabbal du'a'
translation: My Lord, make me one who establishes prayer, and also my descendants. Our Lord, accept my supplication.
benefits: This is Ibrahim's (peace be upon him) prayer for himself and his progeny to be steadfast in salah, and for his supplications to be accepted. It teaches us to pray not only for ourselves but also for our children and future generations.

title: Prayer of the Companions of the Cave
arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ رَحْمَةً وَهَيِّئْ لَنَا مِنْ أَمْرِنَا رَشَدًا
latin: rabbana atina min ladunka rahmatan wa hayyi' lana min amrina rashada
translation: Our Lord, grant us mercy from Yourself and provide us with right guidance in our affairs.
benefits: This is the sincere prayer of the young believers (Ashabul Kahf) who fled to a cave to protect their faith from a tyrant king. Their prayer was answered miraculously — Allah caused them to sleep for centuries and kept their faith safe. Recite this when seeking guidance in difficult decisions.

title: Prayer of Believers for Forgiveness and Mercy
arabic: رَبَّنَا آمَنَّا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَا وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ الرَّاحِمِينَ
latin: rabbana amanna faghfir lana warhamna wa anta khayru ar-rahimin
translation: Our Lord, we have believed, so forgive us and have mercy on us. You are the best of those who show mercy.
benefits: Allah mentions this as the prayer of a group of His servants who were mocked by the disbelievers in this world. On the Day of Judgment, these very believers will be greatly rewarded for their patience and sincerity in faith.

title: Prayer for Protection from the Punishment of Hellfire
arabic: رَبَّنَا اصْرِفْ عَنَّا عَذَابَ جَهَنَّمَ إِنَّ عَذَابَهَا كَانَ غَرَامًا إِنَّهَا سَاءَتْ مُسْتَقَرًّا وَمُقَامًا
latin: rabbana isrif 'anna 'adhaba jahannama inna 'adhabaha kana gharama, innaha sa'at mustaqarran wa muqama
translation: Our Lord, keep the punishment of Hell away from us. Truly its torment clings as an unrelenting burden — indeed it is a miserable resting place and a dreadful abode.
benefits: This is the prayer of the 'Ibad ar-Rahman (servants of the Most Merciful), described with the finest qualities in Surah Al-Furqan. Their deep awareness of the severity of Hell's punishment drives them to earnestly seek Allah's protection.

title: Prayer for Righteous Spouses and Children
arabic: رَبَّنَا هَبْ لَنَا مِنْ أَزْوَاجِنَا وَذُرِّيَّاتِنَا قُرَّةَ أَعْيُنٍ وَاجْعَلْنَا لِلْمُتَّقِينَ إِمَامًا
latin: rabbana hab lana min azwajina wa dhurriyyatina qurrata a'yunin waj'alna lil-muttaqina imama
translation: Our Lord, grant us in our spouses and children the coolness of our eyes, and make us leaders for the righteous.
benefits: Another prayer of the 'Ibad ar-Rahman, asking for a pious family that brings joy through their righteousness, and to be an upright example for the God-fearing. It is one of the most beloved duas for families seeking happiness and divine guidance.

title: Prayer of the Angels for the Believers
arabic: رَبَّنَا وَسِعْتَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ رَّحْمَةً وَعِلْمًا فَاغْفِرْ لِلَّذِينَ تَابُوا وَاتَّبَعُوا سَبِيلَكَ وَقِهِمْ عَذَابَ الْجَحِيمِ رَبَّنَا وَأَدْخِلْهُمْ جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ الَّتِي وَعَدتَّهُمْ وَمَن صَلَحَ مِنْ آبَائِهِمْ وَأَزْوَاجِهِمْ وَذُرِّيَّاتِهِمْ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ وَقِهِمُ السَّيِّئَاتِ وَمَن تَقِ السَّيِّئَاتِ يَوْمَئِذٍ فَقَدْ رَحِمْتَهُ وَذَلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ
latin: rabbana wasi'ta kulla shay'in rahmatan wa 'ilman faghfir lilladhina tabu wattaba'u sabilaka wa qihim 'adhaba al-jahim, rabbana wa adkhilhum jannati 'adnin allati wa'adtahum wa man salaha min aba'ihim wa azwajihim wa dhurriyyatihim innaka antal-'azizul-hakim, wa qihimus-sayyi'at wa man taqi as-sayyi'ati yawma'idhin faqad rahimtahu wa dhalika huwas-fawzul-'adhim
translation: Our Lord, You encompass all things in mercy and knowledge. Forgive those who repent and follow Your path, and save them from the punishment of the blazing Fire. Our Lord, admit them into the Gardens of Eden which You promised them, along with the righteous from their parents, spouses, and offspring. Indeed, You are the Almighty, the All-Wise. Protect them from evil deeds, for whoever You guard from evil on that Day, You have truly shown them mercy — and that is the ultimate success.
benefits: Remarkably, this is the prayer of the angels who carry the Throne, interceding for the believers. It is a comprehensive supplication asking for forgiveness, admission to Paradise for believers and their families, and protection from evil on the Day of Judgment.

title: Prayer for Forgiveness for Oneself and Fellow Believers
arabic: رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لَنَا وَلِإِخْوَانِنَا الَّذِينَ سَبَقُونَا بِالْإِيمَانِ وَلَا تَجْعَلْ فِي قُلُوبِنَا غِلًّا لِّلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ رَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ
latin: rabbana ighfir lana wa li'ikhwaninal-ladhina sabaquna bil-imani wa la taj'al fi qulubina ghillan lilladhina amanu rabbana innaka ra'ufun rahim
translation: Our Lord, forgive us and our brothers who came before us in faith. Do not let our hearts hold any ill feeling toward the believers. Our Lord, You are indeed Most Kind and Merciful.
benefits: This is the prayer of those who came after the Companions, praying for themselves and for all who came before them in faith. It teaches brotherhood in Islam — to pray for past believers and to purify the heart of any rancor or envy toward fellow Muslims.

title: Prayer Not to Be a Trial for the Disbelievers
arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تَجْعَلْنَا فِتْنَةً لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ أَنتَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
latin: rabbana la taj'alna fitnatan lil-ladhina kafaru waghfir lana rabbana innaka antal-'azizul-hakim
translation: Our Lord, do not make us a trial for those who disbelieve, and forgive us. Our Lord, truly You are the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
benefits: This is the prayer of Prophet Ibrahim (peace be upon him) and the believers with him when they separated from their idolatrous people. It asks Allah not to let believers become weak or defeated in a way that would embolden disbelievers, and seeks personal forgiveness.

title: Prayer for Completion of Light and Forgiveness
arabic: رَبَّنَا أَتْمِمْ لَنَا نُورَنَا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا إِنَّكَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
latin: rabbana atmim lana nurana waghfir lana innaka 'ala kulli shay'in qadir
translation: Our Lord, perfect our light for us and forgive us. Truly, You have power over all things.
benefits: This will be the prayer of the believing men and women on the Day of Judgment, when they see their light going ahead of them on the bridge (Sirat). They will ask Allah to complete and perfect their light as a guide to Paradise. It is a dua for divine guidance, forgiveness, and grace.

title: Supplication for a Steadfast Heart
arabic: يَا مُقَلِّبَ الْقُلُوبِ ثَبِّتْ قَلْبِي عَلَى دِينِكَ
latin: ya muqallibal-qulub thabbit qalbi 'ala dinik
translation: O Changer of the hearts, make my heart firm upon Your religion.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ recited this dua frequently. It is one of the most important supplications to protect the heart from deviation after guidance. Umm Salamah reported that he ﷺ used to say it often when he was at home.

title: Supplication for Good Surprises and Protection from Bad Ones
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ فُجَاءَةِ الْخَيْرِ وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ فُجَاءَةِ الشَّرِّ
latin: allahumma inni as'aluka min fuja'atil-khayri wa a'udhu bika min fuja'atish-sharr
translation: O Allah, I ask You for good surprises, and I seek refuge in You from bad surprises.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ used to recite this supplication every morning and evening. A servant never knows what may surprise him throughout his day, so he turns to Allah to ensure that whatever comes unexpectedly brings good and not harm.

title: Supplication for Protection of One's Religion, Life, Family, and Wealth
arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ عَلَى دِينِي وَنَفْسِي وَوَلَدِي وَأَهْلِي وَمَالِي
latin: bismillahi 'ala dini wa nafsi wa waladi wa ahli wa mali
translation: In the name of Allah upon my religion, my life, my children, my family, and my wealth.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ advised reciting this three times every morning and evening. A bedouin once came to the Prophet expressing fear for his life, offspring, family, and wealth — and the Prophet taught him these words as a comprehensive shield of protection.

title: Supplication for Removing Grief and Replacing It with Joy
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي عَبْدُكَ ابْنُ عَبْدِكَ ابْنُ أَمَتِكَ نَاصِيَتِي بِيَدِكَ مَاضٍ فِيَّ حُكْمُكَ عَدْلٌ فِيَّ قَضَاؤُكَ أَسْأَلُكَ بِكُلِّ اسْمٍ هُوَ لَكَ سَمَّيْتَ بِهِ نَفْسَكَ أَوْ أَنْزَلْتَهُ فِي كِتَابِكَ أَوْ عَلَّمْتَهُ أَحَداً مِنْ خَلْقِكَ أَوِ اسْتَأْثَرْتَ بِهِ فِي عِلْمِ الْغَيْبِ عِنْدَكَ أَنْ تَجْعَلَ الْقُرْآنَ رَبِيعَ قَلْبِي وَنُورَ صَدْرِي وَجِلَاءَ حُزْنِي وَذَهَابَ هَمِّي
latin: allahumma inni 'abduka wabnu 'abdika wabnu amatika, nasiyati bi-yadika, madin fiyya hukmuka, 'adlun fiyya qada'uka, as'aluka bi-kulli ismin huwa laka sammayta bihi nafsaka aw anzaltahu fi kitabika aw 'allamtahu ahadan min khalqika aw ista'tharta bihi fi 'ilmil-ghaybi 'indaka, an taj'alal-qur'ana rabi'a qalbi wa nura sadri wa jala'a huzni wa dhahaba hammi
translation: O Allah, I am Your slave, son of Your slave, son of Your maidservant. My forelock is in Your hand, Your decree over me is just, and Your judgment upon me is fair. I ask You by every name that is Yours — with which You named Yourself, or revealed in Your Book, or taught to any of Your creation, or kept in the knowledge of the unseen with You — to make the Quran the spring of my heart, the light of my chest, the removal of my sadness, and the departure of my anxiety.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'There is no one afflicted with distress and grief who reads this dua but Allah will remove his distress and grief and replace it with joy.' He was asked: 'Should we learn it?' He replied: 'Of course — whoever hears it should learn it.'
source: HR. Ahmad No. 3712, Ibn Hibban No. 972

title: Supplication for Sustenance and Freedom from Debt (Taught to Fatimah)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ السَّمَاوَاتِ السَّبْعِ وَرَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ رَبَّنَا وَرَبَّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُنْزِلَ التَّوْرَاةِ وَالْإِنْجِيلِ وَالْقُرْآنِ الْعَظِيمِ أَنْتَ الْأَوَّلُ فَلَيْسَ قَبْلَكَ شَيْءٌ وَأَنْتَ الْآخِرُ فَلَيْسَ بَعْدَكَ شَيْءٌ وَأَنْتَ الظَّاهِرُ فَلَيْسَ فَوْقَكَ شَيْءٌ وَأَنْتَ الْبَاطِنُ فَلَيْسَ دُونَكَ شَيْءٌ اقْضِ عَنَّا الدَّيْنَ وَأَغْنِنَا مِنَ الْفَقْرِ
latin: allahumma rabbas-samawatis-sab'i wa rabbal-'arshil-'azim, rabbana wa rabba kulli shay'in, munzilat-tawrati wal-injili wal-quranil-'azim, antal-awwalu fa laysa qablaka shay', wa antal-akhiru fa laysa ba'daka shay', antaz-zahiru fa laysa fawqaka shay', wa antal-batinu fa laysa dunaka shay', iqdi 'annad-daina wa aghnina minal-faqr
translation: O Allah, Lord of the seven heavens and Lord of the Mighty Throne, our Lord and Lord of everything, Revealer of the Torah, the Gospel, and the Magnificent Quran. You are the First and there is nothing before You; You are the Last and there is nothing after You. You are the Most High and there is nothing above You; You are the Most Near and there is nothing nearer than You. Settle our debts and make us free from poverty.
benefits: This dua was personally taught by the Prophet ﷺ to his beloved daughter Fatimah. It is a comprehensive acknowledgment of Allah's eternal attributes, followed by a humble request for relief from debt and poverty. It is also recommended to recite at bedtime for sustenance.

title: Supplication for Forgiveness and Repentance (Read Abundantly)
arabic: رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَتُبْ عَلَيَّ إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ
latin: rabbighfir li wa tub 'alayya innaka antal-tawwabur-rahim
translation: My Lord, forgive me and accept my repentance. You are the Accepter of repentance, the Most Merciful.
benefits: Ibn Umar narrated that we used to count that the Prophet ﷺ would say this supplication one hundred times in a single gathering. Repeating it abundantly cleanses the heart, draws one closer to Allah, and earns tremendous reward.

title: Supplication for Making Difficulties Easy
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ الْطُفْ بِي فِي تَيْسِيرِ كُلِّ عَسِيرٍ فَإِنَّ تَيْسِيرَ كُلِّ عَسِيرٍ عَلَيْكَ يَسِيرٌ وَأَسْأَلُكَ الْيُسْرَ وَالْمُعَافَاةَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ
latin: allahumma-ltuf bi fi taysiri kulli 'asirin, fa-inna taysira kulli 'asirin 'alayka yasir, wa as'alukal-yusra wal-mu'afata fid-dunya wal-akhirah
translation: O Allah, be gentle with me in making every difficult matter easy, for making every difficult matter easy is easy for You. And I ask You for ease and well-being in this world and the Hereafter.
benefits: This dua was personally taught by the Prophet ﷺ to Sayyidina Ja'far. It covers all types of hardship — acknowledging Allah's limitless power over every difficulty while asking for both ease and well-being in both worlds.
source: HR. narrated by Bayhaqi (taught by the Prophet ﷺ to Ja'far)

title: Supplication for Good Deeds, Love of the Poor, and Protection from Trials
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ فِعْلَ الْخَيْرَاتِ وَتَرْكَ الْمُنْكَرَاتِ وَحُبَّ الْمَسَاكِينِ وَأَنْ تَغْفِرَ لِي وَتَرْحَمَنِي وَإِذَا أَرَدْتَ فِتْنَةً فِي قَوْمٍ فَتَوَفَّنِي غَيْرَ مَفْتُونٍ وَأَسْأَلُكَ حُبَّكَ وَحُبَّ مَنْ يُحِبُّكَ وَحُبَّ عَمَلٍ يُقَرِّبُنِي إِلَى حُبِّكَ
latin: allahumma inni as'aluka fi'lal-khayrati wa tarkal-munkari wa hubbal-masakini wa an taghfira li wa tarhamani wa idha aradta fitnatan fi qawmin fatawaffani ghayra maftunin wa as'aluka hubbaka wa hubba man yuhibbuka wa hubba 'amalin yuqarribuni ila hubbika
translation: O Allah, I ask You for the ability to do good deeds, to avoid evil, and to love the poor. I ask You to forgive me and have mercy on me. If You intend to test a people with tribulation, take me without being tried. I ask You for Your love, the love of those who love You, and the love of deeds that draw me closer to Your love.
benefits: This dua asks for righteous character, love for the poor (a sign of true faith), forgiveness, and — most importantly — the love of Allah. It comprehensively covers the spiritual journey of a believer and was taught by the Prophet ﷺ himself.

title: Supplication Between the Two Prostrations (Jalsah)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَارْحَمْنِي وَعَافِنِي وَاهْدِنِي وَارْزُقْنِي وَاجْبُرْنِي وَارْفَعْنِي
latin: allahumma-ghfir li warhamni wa 'afini wahdini warzuqni wajburni warf'ani
translation: O Allah, forgive me, have mercy on me, grant me well-being, guide me, provide for me, restore what I have lost, and elevate my status.
benefits: This brief yet comprehensive dua is recited between the two prostrations in prayer (jalsah). In just a few words, it asks for seven things: forgiveness, mercy, well-being, guidance, sustenance, compensation for loss, and elevation — making it one of the most complete duas in salah.
source: HR. Abu Dawud No. 850, at-Tirmidhi No. 284

title: Supplication of Khidr for Times of Difficulty
arabic: يَا لَطِيفاً بِخَلْقِهِ يَا عَلِيماً بِخَلْقِهِ يَا خَبِيراً بِخَلْقِهِ الْطُفْ بِي يَا لَطِيفُ يَا عَلِيمُ يَا خَبِيرُ
latin: ya lateefan bi khalqihi ya 'aliman bi khalqihi ya khabiran bi khalqihi, ultuf bi ya lateefu ya 'alimu ya khabiru
translation: O You who is Most Gentle with His creation, O You who is Most Knowing of His creation, O You who is Most Aware of His creation — be gentle with me, O Most Gentle, O All-Knowing, O Most Aware.
benefits: This dua was passed down by scholars as a supplication of relief for times of hardship and danger. It invokes three beautiful names of Allah — Al-Latif (the Most Gentle), Al-'Alim (the All-Knowing), and Al-Khabir (the All-Aware) — affirming that Allah knows and is fully aware of every detail of our situation.

title: Supplication for a Blessed Death
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لِي فِي الْمَوْتِ وَفِيمَا بَعْدَ الْمَوْتِ
latin: allahumma barik li fil-mawti wa fima ba'dal-mawt
translation: O Allah, bless me in death and in what comes after death.
benefits: Death is inevitable for every soul, but the condition of death and the state of the hereafter can vary greatly. A believer should always ask Allah for a blessed and easy death and a peaceful hereafter. This short dua encompasses both the moment of death and all that follows.

title: Supplication for a Living Heart
arabic: يَا حَيُّ يَا قَيُّومُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ
latin: ya hayyu ya qayyum la ilaha illa anta
translation: O Ever-Living, O Self-Subsisting, there is no deity except You.
benefits: Imam Shaatibi narrated that Imam Kattani saw the Prophet ﷺ in a dream and asked him to pray that his heart would not die. The Prophet ﷺ instructed him to say this dua 40 times every day. Al-Hayy (Ever-Living) and Al-Qayyum (Self-Subsisting) are among the greatest names of Allah.

title: Friday Supplication for Lawful Sustenance
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ يَا غَنِيُّ يَا حَمِيدُ يَا مُبْدِئُ يَا مُعِيدُ يَا رَحِيمُ يَا وَدُودُ أَغْنِنِي بِحَلَالِكَ عَنْ حَرَامِكَ وَبِطَاعَتِكَ عَنْ مَعْصِيَتِكَ وَبِفَضْلِكَ عَمَّنْ سِوَاكَ
latin: allahumma ya ghaniyyu ya hamidu ya mubdi'u ya mu'idu ya rahimu ya wadudu, aghnini bi-halalika 'an haramika wa bi-ta'atika 'an ma'siyatika wa bi-fadlika 'amman siwaka
translation: O Allah, O Most Wealthy, O Most Praiseworthy, O Originator, O Restorer, O Most Merciful, O Most Loving — enrich me with what is lawful so I have no need for what is unlawful, with Your obedience so I have no need for Your disobedience, and with Your bounty so I have no need for anyone other than You.
benefits: Mentioned by Imam al-Ghazali in his Ihya Ulum al-Din. Scholars advise reciting this 4 times after the Friday prayer. Whoever recites it, Allah will enrich him from unexpected sources, forgive his past and future sins, and protect his religion and worldly affairs.

title: Supplication for Protection Against Enemies
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّا نَجْعَلُكَ فِي نُحُورِهِمْ وَنَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شُرُورِهِمْ
latin: allahumma inna naj'aluka fi nuhurihim wa na'udhu bika min shururihim
translation: O Allah, we place You before them as our shield, and we seek refuge in You from their evils.
benefits: Whenever the Prophet ﷺ had fear of an enemy, he would recite this supplication. It is a declaration of total reliance on Allah as the ultimate protection, placing Allah Himself as the barrier between the believer and all harm.

title: Supplication for Strength, Honor, and Provision
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي ضَعِيفٌ فَقَوِّنِي فِي رِضَاكَ ضَعْفِي وَخُذْ إِلَى الْخَيْرِ بِنَاصِيَتِي وَاجْعَلِ الْإِسْلَامَ مُنْتَهَى رِضَائِي اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي ضَعِيفٌ فَقَوِّنِي وَإِنِّي ذَلِيلٌ فَأَعِزَّنِي وَإِنِّي فَقِيرٌ فَارْزُقْنِي
latin: allahumma inni da'ifun faqawwini fi ridaka da'fi, wa khudh ilal-khayri bi nasiyati, waj'alil-islama muntaha rida'i, allahumma inni da'ifun faqawwini wa inni dhalilun fa-a'izzani wa inni faqirun farzuqni
translation: O Allah, I am weak so strengthen me to please You in my weakness, take me by my forelock toward all good, and make Islam the utmost of my aspirations. O Allah, I am weak so give me strength; I am humble so ennoble me; and I am poor so provide for me.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ taught this dua to the Companion Buraydah, saying: 'Shall I not teach you words that Allah teaches to the one for whom He wants good, and then never allows him to forget?' It beautifully acknowledges one's weakness before Allah and asks for the three greatest needs of a believer: strength, honor, and provision.
source: HR. al-Hakim and Ibn Abi Shaybah

title: Comprehensive Morning & Evening Supplication for Well-Being
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْعَفْوَ وَالْعَافِيَةَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْعَفْوَ وَالْعَافِيَةَ فِي دِينِي وَدُنْيَايَ وَأَهْلِي وَمَالِي، اللَّهُمَّ اسْتُرْ عَوْرَاتِي وَآمِنْ رَوْعَاتِي، اللَّهُمَّ احْفَظْنِي مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيَّ وَمِنْ خَلْفِي وَعَنْ يَمِينِي وَعَنْ شِمَالِي وَمِنْ فَوْقِي، وَأَعُوذُ بِعَظَمَتِكَ أَنْ أُغْتَالَ مِنْ تَحْتِي
latin: allahumma inni as'alukal-'afwa wal-'afiyata fid-dunya wal-akhirah, allahumma inni as'alukal-'afwa wal-'afiyata fi dini wa dunyaya wa ahli wa mali, allahummastur 'awrati wa amin raw'ati, allahummahfazni min bayni yadayya wa min khalfi wa 'an yamini wa 'an shimali wa min fawqi, wa a'udhu bi'azamatika an ughtala min tahti
translation: O Allah, I ask You for pardon and well-being in this world and the Hereafter. O Allah, I ask You for pardon and well-being in my religion, my worldly affairs, my family and my wealth. O Allah, conceal my faults and calm my fears. O Allah, guard me from in front of me and from behind me, from my right, from my left, and from above me. I seek refuge in Your greatness from being struck from below.
notes: Recited with the same wording in both morning and evening — no word changes required.
benefits: Abdullah ibn Umar narrated that the Messenger ﷺ never failed to say these words in the morning and in the evening. The Messenger ﷺ also said: 'Ask Allah for forgiveness and well-being (afiyah). Indeed, after conviction (yaqin), no one has been granted anything better than well-being.' This comprehensive dua covers all dimensions of life.
source: HR. Abu Dawud No. 5074, at-Tirmidhi No. 3514

title: Morning & Evening Supplication Seeking Allah's Mercy to Rectify All Affairs
arabic: يَا حَيُّ يَا قَيُّومُ بِرَحْمَتِكَ أَسْتَغِيثُ، أَصْلِحْ لِي شَأْنِي كُلَّهُ وَلَا تَكِلْنِي إِلَى نَفْسِي طَرْفَةَ عَيْنٍ
latin: ya hayyu ya qayyum bi rahmatika astaghith, aslih li sha'ni kullahu wa la takilni ila nafsi tarfata 'ayn
translation: O Ever-Living, O Self-Sustaining and All-Sustaining, I seek help through Your mercy. Rectify all of my affairs and do not entrust me to myself for the blink of an eye.
notes: Recited with the same wording in both morning and evening — no word changes required.
benefits: Anas reported that whenever a serious matter distressed the Messenger ﷺ, he would say this supplication. The Messenger ﷺ also said: 'Supplicate frequently with: O Possessor of Majesty and Honour.' Al-Hayy (Ever-Living) and Al-Qayyum (Self-Sustaining) are among the greatest names of Allah — recognising that all our affairs depend entirely on His mercy.
source: HR. al-Hakim, Ahmad No. 10405, an-Nasa'i (Amal al-Yawm wal-Laylah)

title: Morning & Evening: Acknowledging All Blessings Come from Allah
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ مَا أَصْبَحَ (أَمْسَى) بِي مِنْ نِعْمَةٍ أَوْ بِأَحَدٍ مِنْ خَلْقِكَ فَمِنْكَ وَحْدَكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ، فَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ وَلَكَ الشُّكْرُ
latin: allahumma ma asbaha (amsa) bi min ni'mah aw bi ahadin min khalqika faminka wahdaka la sharika lak, falakal-hamdu wa lakas-shukr
translation: O Allah, whatever blessing I or any of Your creation have received this morning (evening) is from You alone. You have no partner. To You belongs all praise and all thanks.
notes: Morning: أَصْبَحَ (asbaha) / Evening: أَمْسَى (amsa) — use the word in parentheses for evening.
benefits: The Messenger ﷺ said: 'Whoever says this in the morning has fulfilled his obligation of thanks to Allah for that day. And whoever says it in the evening has fulfilled his obligation for that night.' This simple acknowledgment is a complete fulfilment of daily gratitude to Allah.
source: HR. Abu Dawud No. 5073, Ibn al-Sunni

title: Morning & Evening: Entrusting Life and Death to Allah
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ وَإِلَيْكَ النُّشُورُ
latin: allahumma bika asbahna wa bika amsayna wa bika nahya wa namutu wa ilaykan-nushur
translation: O Allah, by Your will we enter the morning and by Your will we enter the evening. By Your will we live and by Your will we die, and to You is the resurrection.
notes: Morning: اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ وَإِلَيْكَ النُّشُورُ (Allahumma bika asbahna wa bika amsayna wa bika nahya wa namutu wa ilaykan-nushur) | Evening: اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا وَبِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ (Allahumma bika amsayna wa bika asbahna wa bika nahya wa namutu wa ilayka al-masir) — In the evening, أَمْسَيْنَا comes first and النُّشُورُ (resurrection) changes to الْمَصِيرُ (return).
benefits: This supplication is a declaration that all of life — morning and evening, life and death — belongs entirely to Allah. Reciting it at the start and end of each day renews one's tawakkul (complete reliance) on Allah and affirms that the resurrection is inevitable.
source: HR. at-Tirmidhi No. 3391, Abu Dawud No. 5068

title: Morning & Evening Supplication for Physical and Spiritual Well-Being
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ عَافِنِي فِي بَدَنِي، اللَّهُمَّ عَافِنِي فِي سَمْعِي، اللَّهُمَّ عَافِنِي فِي بَصَرِي، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْكُفْرِ وَالْفَقْرِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ
latin: allahumma 'afini fi badani, allahumma 'afini fi sam'i, allahumma 'afini fi basari, la ilaha illa anta, allahumma inni a'udhu bika minal-kufri wal-faqri, wa a'udhu bika min 'adhabil-qabr, la ilaha illa anta
translation: O Allah, grant me well-being in my body. O Allah, grant me well-being in my hearing. O Allah, grant me well-being in my sight. There is no god but You. O Allah, I seek Your protection from disbelief and poverty, and I seek Your protection from the punishment of the grave. There is no god but You.
notes: Recited with the same wording in both morning and evening — no word changes required.
benefits: This supplication covers physical health (body, hearing, sight) and spiritual protection (from disbelief, poverty, and the punishment of the grave). Reciting it every morning and evening is a means of seeking complete well-being in all aspects of life.

title: Morning & Evening: Declaration of Being Pleased with Allah, Islam, and the Prophet (3x)
arabic: رَضِيتُ بِاللهِ رَبًّا وَبِالْإِسْلَامِ دِينًا وَبِمُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نَبِيًّا
latin: raditu billahi rabban wa bil-islami dinan wa bi-muhammadin sallallahu 'alayhi wa sallama nabiyyan
translation: I am pleased with Allah as my Lord, with Islam as my religion, and with Muhammad ﷺ as my Prophet.
notes: Recited with the same wording in both morning and evening — no word changes required.
benefits: The Messenger ﷺ said: 'Allah has promised that anyone who says this three times every morning and evening will be pleased on the Day of Judgement.' In another narration: 'Whoever says this in the morning, I guarantee that I will take hold of his hand until I enter him into Paradise.' A simple yet powerful declaration of complete contentment with one's faith.

title: Morning & Evening: Protection in the Name of Allah from All Harm (3x)
arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ الَّذِي لَا يَضُرُّ مَعَ اسْمِهِ شَيْءٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
latin: bismillahilladhi la yadurru ma'asmihi shay'un fil-ardi wa la fis-sama'i wa huwas-sami'ul-'alim
translation: In the name of Allah, with whose Name nothing in the earth or the sky can cause harm. He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
notes: Recited with the same wording in both morning and evening — no word changes required.
benefits: The Messenger ﷺ said: 'Whoever recites this three times every morning and evening, nothing will harm him.' It is one of the most powerful adhkar of the morning and evening, shielding the believer from all forms of physical and spiritual harm through the barakah of Allah's name.
source: HR. at-Tirmidhi No. 3388, Abu Dawud No. 5088

title: Morning & Evening: Trusting Completely in Allah (7x)
arabic: حَسْبِيَ اللهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَهُوَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ
latin: hasbiyallahu la ilaha illa huwa 'alayhi tawakkaltu wa huwa rabbul-'arshil-'azim
translation: Allah is sufficient for me. There is no god but Him. I have placed my trust in Him, and He is the Lord of the Magnificent Throne.
notes: Recited with the same wording in both morning and evening — no word changes required.
benefits: The Messenger ﷺ said: 'Whoever says this seven times in the morning and seven times in the evening, Allah will suffice him in all that concerns him.' This declaration combines complete surrender (tawakkul) with acknowledgment of Allah's Lordship over the Magnificent Throne, making it one of the most reassuring morning and evening adhkar.
source: HR. Abu Dawud No. 5081, Ibn al-Sunni

title: Morning & Evening: Calling All Creation to Witness Allah's Oneness (4x)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَصْبَحْتُ (أَمْسَيْتُ) أُشْهِدُكَ وَأُشْهِدُ حَمَلَةَ عَرْشِكَ وَمَلَائِكَتَكَ وَجَمِيعَ خَلْقِكَ أَنَّكَ أَنْتَ اللهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ وَحْدَكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ وَأَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا عَبْدُكَ وَرَسُولُكَ
latin: allahumma inni asbahtu (amsaytu) ush-hiduka wa ush-hidu hamalata 'arshika wa mala'ikataka wa jami'a khalqika annaka antallahu la ilaha illa anta wahdaka la sharika laka wa anna muhammadan 'abduka wa rasuluk
translation: O Allah, I have entered the morning (evening) and I call upon You to bear witness, and I call upon the bearers of Your Throne, Your angels, and all of Your creation to bear witness: that You are Allah. There is no god but You alone. You have no partner. And that Muhammad is Your slave and Your Messenger.
notes: Morning: أَصْبَحْتُ (asbahtu) / Evening: أَمْسَيْتُ (amsaytu) — use the word in parentheses for evening.
benefits: The Messenger ﷺ said: 'Whoever reads this once in the morning or evening, Allah frees a quarter of him from the Hellfire. If read twice, half. If three times, three-quarters. And if four times, Allah completely frees him from the Hellfire.' This makes it one of the most remarkably rewarding of the morning and evening supplications.
source: HR. Abu Dawud No. 5069, at-Tirmidhi No. 3501

title: Evening Supplication: Seeking Protection from the Evil of Allah's Creation (3x)
arabic: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
latin: a'udhu bi kalimati allahi at-tammat min sharri ma khalaqa
translation: I seek protection in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of all that He created.
benefits: The Messenger ﷺ said: 'Whoever recites this three times in the evening will be protected from insect stings that night.' This supplication is a comprehensive shield against all created harm — recited specifically in the evening as protection through the night.

title: Morning & Evening: The Greatest Tasbih (3x)
arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ عَدَدَ خَلْقِهِ وَرِضَا نَفْسِهِ وَزِنَةَ عَرْشِهِ وَمِدَادَ كَلِمَاتِهِ
latin: subhanallahi wa bihamdihi 'adada khalqihi wa rida nafsihi wa zinata 'arshihi wa midada kalimatihi
translation: Glory be to Allah and all praise is due to Him, as many times as the number of His creation, as much as pleases Him, as weighty as His Throne, and as endless as the ink of His words.
notes: Recited with the same wording in both morning and evening — no word changes required.
benefits: Juwayriyah bint al-Harith reported that the Prophet ﷺ left her home after Fajr and returned at mid-morning to find her still in dhikr. He ﷺ said: 'I recited four phrases three times after I left you. If everything that you have said today was placed on the scales, these phrases would outweigh it all.' Reciting it three times surpasses hours of ordinary tasbih.

title: Supplication Before Sleeping: Entrusting Sleep to Allah
arabic: بِاسْمِكَ رَبِّي وَضَعْتُ جَنْبِي وَبِكَ أَرْفَعُهُ، فَإِنْ أَمْسَكْتَ نَفْسِي فَارْحَمْهَا، وَإِنْ أَرْسَلْتَهَا فَاحْفَظْهَا بِمَا تَحْفَظُ بِهِ عِبَادَكَ الصَّالِحِينَ
latin: bismika rabbi wada'tu janbi wa bika arfa'uh, fa in amsakta nafsi farhamha, wa in arsaltaha fahfazha bima tahfazu bihi 'ibadakas-salihin
translation: In Your name, my Lord, I lie down, and in Your name I rise. If You take my soul, have mercy upon it. And if You return it, protect it with what You protect Your righteous servants.
benefits: This dua acknowledges that sleep is a minor form of death — the soul is taken by Allah and returned upon waking. By reciting it, the believer surrenders their soul to Allah entirely, asking for mercy if death comes and protection if they are granted another day.

title: Supplication Before Sleeping: Protection from the Punishment on the Day of Resurrection (3x)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ قِنِي عَذَابَكَ يَوْمَ تَبْعَثُ عِبَادَكَ
latin: allahumma qini 'adhabaka yawma tab'athu 'ibadak
translation: O Allah, protect me from Your punishment on the Day You resurrect Your servants.
benefits: This supplication is recited three times before sleeping. It serves as a nightly reminder of the Day of Resurrection and a sincere request for divine protection — motivating the believer to end each day having done righteous deeds.

title: Supplication Before Sleeping: Complete Submission and Trust in Allah
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَسْلَمْتُ نَفْسِي إِلَيْكَ وَفَوَّضْتُ أَمْرِي إِلَيْكَ وَوَجَّهْتُ وَجْهِي إِلَيْكَ وَأَلْجَأْتُ ظَهْرِي إِلَيْكَ رَغْبَةً وَرَهْبَةً إِلَيْكَ لَا مَلْجَأَ وَلَا مَنْجَا مِنْكَ إِلَّا إِلَيْكَ آمَنْتُ بِكِتَابِكَ الَّذِي أَنْزَلْتَ وَبِنَبِيِّكَ الَّذِي أَرْسَلْتَ
latin: allahumma aslamtu nafsi ilayk wa fawwadtu amri ilayk wa wajjahtu wajhi ilayk wa alja'tu zahri ilayk, raghbatan wa rahbatan ilayk, la malja'a wa la manja minka illa ilayk, amantu bi kitabikal-ladhi anzalta wa bi nabiyyikal-ladhi arsalt
translation: O Allah, I submit my soul to You, entrust my affairs to You, turn my face toward You, and lean entirely on You, in hope and fear of You. There is no refuge or salvation from You except with You. I believe in Your Book which You revealed and in Your Prophet whom You sent.
benefits: The Messenger ﷺ said: 'Whoever says this before sleeping and dies in their sleep will die upon the fitrah (pure natural state of Islam).' It is a complete declaration of submission to Allah — in hope and fear — making it among the most important supplications before sleep.

title: Supplication Before Sleeping: Gratitude for Food, Drink, and Shelter
arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَطْعَمَنَا وَسَقَانَا وَكَفَانَا وَآوَانَا، فَكَمْ مِمَّنْ لَا كَافِيَ لَهُ وَلَا مُؤْوِيَ
latin: alhamdulillahilladhi at'amana wa saqana wa kafana wa awana, fakam mimman la kafiya lahu wa la mu'wiy
translation: All praise is for Allah who has fed us, given us to drink, sufficed us, and sheltered us. For how many are there who have none to suffice them or shelter them?
benefits: The Messenger ﷺ said: 'Whoever says this when retiring to bed has praised Allah with all the praises of His entire creation.' It closes the day with gratitude for the basic blessings of food, drink, and shelter — while reflecting with humility on those who lack them.

title: Supplication After Each Obligatory Prayer: Seeking Forgiveness and Praising Allah
arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللهَ، أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللهَ، أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللهَ، اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ السَّلَامُ وَمِنْكَ السَّلَامُ تَبَارَكْتَ يَا ذَا الْجَلَالِ وَالْإِكْرَامِ
latin: astaghfirullah, astaghfirullah, astaghfirullah, allahumma antas-salam wa minkas-salam, tabarakta ya dhal-jalali wal-ikram
translation: I seek the forgiveness of Allah (x3). O Allah, You are The Flawless and The Source of Peace, and from You comes peace. Blessed are You, full of Majesty and Honour.
benefits: This is the first dhikr recited after every obligatory prayer. Saying 'Astaghfirullah' three times immediately after prayer acknowledges that even in worship we fall short. Allah is then glorified as As-Salam — the Source of all Peace and Perfection.

title: Supplication After Each Prayer: Asking for Help to Remember Allah, Be Grateful, and Worship Well
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَعِنِّي عَلَى ذِكْرِكَ وَشُكْرِكَ وَحُسْنِ عِبَادَتِكَ
latin: allahumma a'inni 'ala dhikrika wa shukrika wa husni 'ibadatik
translation: O Allah, help me to remember You, to be grateful to You, and to worship You in the best manner.
benefits: Mu'adh ibn Jabal reported that the Messenger ﷺ took his hand and said: 'O Mu'adh, I love you. So never forget to say after every prayer: O Allah, help me to remember You, to thank You, and to worship You in the best manner.' This makes it among the most personally recommended supplications the Prophet ﷺ taught.
source: HR. Abu Dawud No. 1522, an-Nasa'i No. 1303

title: Supplication After Each Prayer: Seeking Protection from Four Serious Harms
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْجُبْنِ وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ أَنْ أُرَدَّ إِلَى أَرْذَلِ الْعُمُرِ وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ فِتْنَةِ الدُّنْيَا وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ
latin: allahumma inni a'udhu bika minal-jubni wa a'udhu bika an uradda ila ardhalil-'umuri wa a'udhu bika min fitnatid-dunya wa a'udhu bika min 'adhabill-qabr
translation: O Allah, I seek Your protection from cowardice. I seek Your protection from being reduced to the worst stage of old age. I seek Your protection from the trials of the world. And I seek Your protection from the punishment of the grave.
benefits: Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas reported that the Messenger ﷺ used to seek refuge from these four things after every prayer. Together they cover the most feared conditions: spiritual weakness (cowardice), physical decline (senility), worldly temptations, and punishment in the grave.

title: Supplication After Fajr and Maghrib: Protection from the Hellfire (7x)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَجِرْنِي مِنَ النَّارِ
latin: allahumma ajirni minan-nar
translation: O Allah, protect me from the Hellfire.
benefits: The Messenger ﷺ said: 'When you finish Fajr prayer, say this seven times before speaking. If you die that day, Allah will protect you from the Fire. And after Maghrib prayer, say the same. If you die that night, Allah will protect you from the Fire.' Its simplicity belies its tremendous reward.

title: Supplication After Fajr Prayer: Asking for Beneficial Knowledge, Wholesome Provision, and Accepted Deeds
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ عِلْمًا نَافِعًا وَرِزْقًا طَيِّبًا وَعَمَلًا مُتَقَبَّلًا
latin: allahumma inni as'aluka 'ilman nafi'an wa rizqan tayyiban wa 'amalan mutaqabbala
translation: O Allah, I ask You for beneficial knowledge, wholesome provision, and accepted deeds.
benefits: Recited specifically after the Fajr prayer, this dua is a perfect start to every day. It asks for the three most important things a believer needs: knowledge that benefits in this life and the next, provision that is pure and blessed, and deeds that are accepted by Allah.

title: Supplication for Seeking Allah's Guidance in Making a Decision (Istikharah)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْتَخِيرُكَ بِعِلْمِكَ وَأَسْتَقْدِرُكَ بِقُدْرَتِكَ وَأَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ الْعَظِيمِ فَإِنَّكَ تَقْدِرُ وَلَا أَقْدِرُ وَتَعْلَمُ وَلَا أَعْلَمُ وَأَنْتَ عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنْ كُنْتَ تَعْلَمُ أَنَّ هَذَا الْأَمْرَ خَيْرٌ لِي فِي دِينِي وَمَعَاشِي وَعَاقِبَةِ أَمْرِي فَاقْدُرْهُ لِي وَيَسِّرْهُ لِي ثُمَّ بَارِكْ لِي فِيهِ، وَإِنْ كُنْتَ تَعْلَمُ أَنَّ هَذَا الْأَمْرَ شَرٌّ لِي فِي دِينِي وَمَعَاشِي وَعَاقِبَةِ أَمْرِي فَاصْرِفْهُ عَنِّي وَاصْرِفْنِي عَنْهُ وَاقْدُرْ لِيَ الْخَيْرَ حَيْثُ كَانَ ثُمَّ أَرْضِنِي بِهِ
latin: allahumma inni astakhiruka bi'ilmika wa astaqdiruka biqudratika wa as'aluka min fadlikal-'azim, fa innaka taqdiru wa la aqdiru wa ta'lamu wa la a'lamu wa anta 'allamu al-ghuyub, allahumma in kunta ta'lamu anna hadhal-amra khayrun li fi dini wa ma'ashi wa 'aqibati amri faqdiruhu li wa yassirhu li thumma barik li fihi, wa in kunta ta'lamu anna hadhal-amra sharrun li fi dini wa ma'ashi wa 'aqibati amri fasrifhu 'anni wasrifni 'anhu waqdur liyal-khayra haythu kana thumma ardini bih
translation: O Allah, I seek Your guidance by Your knowledge, and I seek Your ability by Your power, and I ask You from Your great bounty. For You are able while I am not, and You know while I do not, and You are the Knower of the unseen. O Allah, if You know that this matter is good for me in my religion, my livelihood, and the outcome of my affairs, then decree it for me, make it easy for me, and bless it for me. But if You know that this matter is bad for me in my religion, my livelihood, and the outcome of my affairs, then turn it away from me, and turn me away from it and decree goodness for me wherever it may be — then make me content with it.
benefits: Jabir narrated: 'The Messenger ﷺ used to teach us istikharah for every matter the same way he taught us surahs from the Quran.' Before any important decision, one prays two voluntary units (rak'ah) of prayer, then sincerely recites this supplication, leaving the outcome entirely to Allah's perfect knowledge and wisdom.
source: HR. al-Bukhari No. 1166, Abu Dawud No. 1538

title: Supplication When Going to the Masjid: Asking Allah for Light in All Directions
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْ فِي قَلْبِي نُورًا وَفِي بَصَرِي نُورًا وَفِي سَمْعِي نُورًا وَعَنْ يَمِينِي نُورًا وَعَنْ يَسَارِي نُورًا وَفَوْقِي نُورًا وَتَحْتِي نُورًا وَأَمَامِي نُورًا وَخَلْفِي نُورًا وَاجْعَلْ لِي نُورًا
latin: allahumma ij'al fi qalbi nuran wa fi basari nuran wa fi sam'i nuran, wa 'an yamini nuran wa 'an yasari nuran, wa fawqi nuran wa tahti nuran, wa amami nuran wa khalfi nuran, waj'al li nuran
translation: O Allah, put light in my heart, light in my sight, and light in my hearing. Put light on my right and light on my left. Put light above me and light beneath me. Put light in front of me, light behind me, and grant me light.
benefits: This dua is recited when walking to the masjid. The Prophet ﷺ asked for light in all directions — physical and spiritual. Light in the heart means guidance; light in the senses means purity of perception; and light in all directions means protection and guidance throughout one's entire life.

title: Supplication for Relief from Physical Pain
arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ (ثَلَاثًا)، أَعُوذُ بِاللهِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَجِدُ وَأُحَاذِرُ
latin: bismillah (3x), a'udhu billahi wa qudratihi min sharri ma ajidu wa uhadhiru (7x)
translation: In the name of Allah (3x). I seek protection in Allah and in His power from the evil of what I feel and from what I fear (7x).
benefits: 'Uthman ibn Abi al-'As complained to the Messenger ﷺ about pain he had felt in his body since accepting Islam. The Messenger ﷺ said: 'Put your hand on the part of your body where you feel pain and say this.' He said: 'I did that and Allah removed my pain. After that, I used to instruct my family and others to do the same.'

title: Supplication When Visiting the Sick (7x)
arabic: أَسْأَلُ اللهَ الْعَظِيمَ رَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ أَنْ يَشْفِيَكَ
latin: as'alullahal-'azima rabbal-'arshil-'azimi an yashfiyak
translation: I ask Allah, the Supreme, Lord of the Magnificent Throne, to cure you.
benefits: The Messenger ﷺ said: 'Whoever visits a sick person who is not on the verge of death and supplicates this seven times, Allah will certainly heal him from that sickness.' Additionally, whoever visits the sick is rewarded with 70,000 angels praying for him until evening.
source: HR. at-Tirmidhi No. 2083, Abu Dawud No. 3106

title: Supplication for Relief from Grief and Anxiety
arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللهُ الْعَظِيمُ الْحَلِيمُ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللهُ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللهُ رَبُّ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَرَبُّ الْأَرْضِ وَرَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْكَرِيمِ
latin: la ilaha illallahu al-'azimul-halim, la ilaha illallahu rabbul-'arshil-'azim, la ilaha illallahu rabbus-samawati wa rabbul-ardi wa rabbul-'arshil-karim
translation: There is no god but Allah, the Supreme, the Forbearing. There is no god but Allah, Lord of the Magnificent Throne. There is no god but Allah, Lord of the heavens, Lord of the earth, and Lord of the Noble Throne.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ used to recite these words when faced with a serious matter or distress. The repeated declaration of Allah's oneness combined with His greatest attributes — Al-'Azim (Supreme), Al-Halim (Forbearing), Rabb (Lord) of the Throne, heavens, and earth — brings calm and reminds the believer of Allah's ultimate, encompassing control.

title: Supplication for Protection from Serious Diseases
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْبَرَصِ وَالْجُنُونِ وَالْجُذَامِ وَمِنْ سَيِّئِ الْأَسْقَامِ
latin: allahumma inni a'udhu bika minal-barasi wal-jununi wal-judhami wa min sayyi'il-asqam
translation: O Allah, I seek Your protection from leprosy, madness, vitiligo, and from serious diseases.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ used to regularly seek refuge from specific incurable and socially debilitating diseases. Reciting this supplication is a means of seeking Allah's protection against all severe illnesses — those known and unknown — and reflects the believer's complete dependence on Allah for health.

title: Surah Al-Fatiha — The Opening (Complete)
arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ ﴿١﴾ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ ﴿٢﴾ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ ﴿٣﴾ مَالِكِ يَوْمِ الدِّينِ ﴿٤﴾ إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ وَإِيَّاكَ نَسْتَعِينُ ﴿٥﴾ اهْدِنَا الصِّرَاطَ الْمُسْتَقِيمَ ﴿٦﴾ صِرَاطَ الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ غَيْرِ الْمَغْضُوبِ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا الضَّالِّينَ ﴿٧﴾
latin: bismillahir-rahmanir-rahim. alhamdulillahi rabbil-'alamin. ar-rahmanir-rahim. maliki yawmid-din. iyyaka na'budu wa iyyaka nasta'in. ihdinas-siratal-mustaqim. siratal-ladhina an'amta 'alayhim ghayril-maghdubi 'alayhim wa lad-dallin
translation: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. (1) All praise is for Allah, Lord of all the worlds. (2) The Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. (3) Master of the Day of Judgment. (4) You alone we worship, and You alone we ask for help. (5) Guide us to the straight path — (6) the path of those You have blessed, not of those who have earned anger, nor of those who are astray. (7)
benefits: Al-Fatiha is the greatest surah of the Quran. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'There is no valid prayer without Al-Fatiha.' It is also a powerful ruqyah (healing recitation): a companion recited it over a man bitten by a scorpion and he was cured — the Prophet ﷺ confirmed it and said, 'How did you know it is a ruqyah?' (Bukhari). Recite it at the start of every prayer, morning and evening, and for healing.
