title: Ayat al-Kursi and the Verses of Protection (Al-Baqarah 255-257)
arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ لَا تَأْخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ وَلَا نَوْمٌ لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ مَن ذَا الَّذِي يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَيْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِ إِلَّا بِمَا شَاءَ وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَلَا يَئُودُهُ حِفْظُهُمَا وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْعَظِيمُ ﴿٢٥٥﴾ لَا إِكْرَاهَ فِي الدِّينِ قَد تَّبَيَّنَ الرُّشْدُ مِنَ الْغَيِّ فَمَن يَكْفُرْ بِالطَّاغُوتِ وَيُؤْمِن بِاللَّهِ فَقَدِ اسْتَمْسَكَ بِالْعُرْوَةِ الْوُثْقَى لَا انفِصَامَ لَهَا وَاللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ ﴿٢٥٦﴾ اللَّهُ وَلِيُّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا يُخْرِجُهُم مِّنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى النُّورِ وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَوْلِيَاؤُهُمُ الطَّاغُوتُ يُخْرِجُونَهُم مِّنَ النُّورِ إِلَى الظُّلُمَاتِ أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ ﴿٢٥٧﴾
latin: allahu la ilaha illa huwal-hayyul-qayyum, la ta'khudhuhu sinatun wa la nawm, lahu ma fis-samawati wa ma fil-ard, man dhal-ladhi yashfa'u 'indahu illa bi-idhnih, ya'lamu ma bayna aydihim wa ma khalfahum, wa la yuhituna bi-shay'in min 'ilmihi illa bima sha', wasi'a kursiyyuhus-samawati wal-ard, wa la ya'uduhu hifdhuhuma wa huwal-'aliyyul-'adhim. la ikraha fid-din, qad tabayyanar-rushdu minal-ghayy, faman yakfur bit-taghuti wa yu'min billahi faqadis-tamsaka bil-'urwatil-wuthqa lan-fisama laha wallahu sami'un 'alim. allahu waliyyul-ladhina amanu yukhrijuhum minadh-dhulumati ilan-nur, wal-ladhina kafaru awliya'uhumut-taghut yukhrijunahum minan-nuri ilad-dhulumat, ula'ika ashhabun-nar, hum fiha khalidun
translation: Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of all existence. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is it that can intercede with Him except by His permission? He knows what is before them and what will be after them, and they cannot encompass a thing of His knowledge except for what He wills. His Throne extends over the heavens and the earth, and their preservation tires Him not. And He is the Most High, the Most Great. (255) There shall be no compulsion in religion. The right way has become distinct from the wrong way. Whoever disbelieves in false deities and believes in Allah has grasped the most trustworthy handhold with no break in it. And Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing. (256) Allah is the ally of those who believe. He brings them out from darknesses into the light. And those who disbelieve — their allies are false deities that take them out of the light into darknesses. Those are the companions of the Fire; they will abide therein eternally. (257)
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi (verse 255) after every obligatory prayer, nothing stands between him and entering Paradise except death.' (Ibn Hibban, authenticated). He also said: 'Recite it before sleeping — a guardian from Allah will be appointed over you and Satan will not come near you until morning.' (Bukhari). These three verses together form the most powerful protection passage in the Quran.

title: Supplication of Complete Reliance upon Allah (Verse of Sufficiency)
arabic: فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا فَقُلْ حَسْبِيَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَهُوَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ
latin: fa-in tawallow faqul hasbiyallahu la ilaha illa huwa, 'alayhi tawakkaltu wa huwa rabbul-'arshil-'adhim
translation: But if they turn away, say: 'Sufficient for me is Allah; there is no deity except Him. Upon Him I have relied, and He is the Lord of the Great Throne.'
benefits: Ibn Abbas reported that when Prophet Ibrahim (peace be upon him) was thrown into the fire, he said 'hasbiyallah wa ni'mal wakeel.' And when the believers were warned 'the people have gathered against you,' they said the same words. Abu Dawud narrated that whoever recites this verse seven times in the morning and seven times in the evening, Allah will suffice him in whatever concerns him of this world and the Hereafter.

title: The Three Concluding Verses of Surah Al-Hashr — Allah's Beautiful Names
arabic: هُوَ اللَّهُ الَّذِي لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ عَالِمُ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ هُوَ الرَّحْمَنُ الرَّحِيمُ ﴿٢٢﴾ هُوَ اللَّهُ الَّذِي لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْمَلِكُ الْقُدُّوسُ السَّلَامُ الْمُؤْمِنُ الْمُهَيْمِنُ الْعَزِيزُ الْجَبَّارُ الْمُتَكَبِّرُ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ ﴿٢٣﴾ هُوَ اللَّهُ الْخَالِقُ الْبَارِئُ الْمُصَوِّرُ لَهُ الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَى يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ ﴿٢٤﴾
latin: huwal-lahulladhi la ilaha illa huwa, 'alimul-ghaybi wash-shahadah, huwar-rahmanur-rahim. huwal-lahulladhi la ilaha illa huwal-malikul-quddusu-s-salamul-mu'minul-muhayminu-l-'azizul-jabbarul-mutakabbir, subhanallahi 'amma yushrikun. huwal-lahul-khaliqul-bari'ul-musawwiru lahul-asma'ul-husna, yusabbihu lahu ma fis-samawati wal-ard, wa huwal-'azizul-hakim
translation: He is Allah, other than Whom there is no deity, Knower of the unseen and the witnessed. He is the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. (22) He is Allah, other than Whom there is no deity, the Sovereign, the Pure, the Giver of Peace, the Granter of Security, the Guardian, the Exalted in Might, the Compeller, the Superior. Exalted is Allah above whatever they associate with Him. (23) He is Allah, the Creator, the Originator, the Fashioner; to Him belong the most beautiful names. Whatever is in the heavens and earth glorifies Him. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise. (24)
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever recites the last three verses of Surah Al-Hashr in the morning — if he dies that day, he dies as a martyr. If he recites them in the evening and dies that night, he dies as a martyr.' (at-Tirmidhi, authenticated by al-Albani). These verses contain 14 of Allah's most beautiful names and should be recited in the morning and evening.

title: Morning & Evening: Allah's Sovereignty Over All (Asbahna wa Asbahal-Mulku Lillah)
arabic: أَصْبَحْنَا (أَمْسَيْنَا) وَأَصْبَحَ (وَأَمْسَى) الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ رَبِّ أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ مَا فِي هَذَا الْيَوْمِ (اللَّيْلَةِ) وَخَيْرَ مَا بَعْدَهُ وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا فِي هَذَا الْيَوْمِ (اللَّيْلَةِ) وَشَرِّ مَا بَعْدَهُ رَبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْكَسَلِ وَسُوءِ الْكِبَرِ رَبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابٍ فِي النَّارِ وَعَذَابٍ فِي الْقَبْرِ
latin: asbahna (amsayna) wa asbahal (wa amsal) mulku lillah, wal-hamdu lillah, la ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamd, wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadir. rabbi as'aluka khayra ma fi hadhal-yawm (al-laylah) wa khayra ma ba'dah, wa a'udhu bika min sharri ma fi hadhal-yawm (al-laylah) wa sharri ma ba'dah. rabbi a'udhu bika minal-kasal wa su'il-kibar. rabbi a'udhu bika min 'adhabin fin-nar wa 'adhabin fil-qabr
translation: We have entered the morning (evening) and the dominion belongs to Allah. All praise is for Allah. There is no god but Allah, alone, with no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is over all things powerful. My Lord, I ask You for the good of this day (night) and the good of what comes after it, and I seek refuge in You from the evil of this day (night) and the evil of what comes after it. My Lord, I seek refuge in You from laziness and the misery of old age. My Lord, I seek refuge in You from the punishment of the Fire and the punishment of the grave.
notes: Morning: أَصْبَحْنَا / وَأَصْبَحَ / هَذَا الْيَوْمِ (asbahna / wa asbahal / hadhal-yawm). Evening: replace with أَمْسَيْنَا / وَأَمْسَى / اللَّيْلَةِ (amsayna / wa amsal / al-laylah) — words in parentheses are the evening alternatives.
benefits: This comprehensive morning and evening supplication covers three pillars: (1) Declaration of Allah's absolute sovereignty, (2) Request for all good of the day/night ahead, and (3) Seeking refuge from laziness, miserable old age, and punishment in the Fire and grave. It is among the most complete of the prophetic morning and evening adhkar.

title: Morning & Evening: Reciting Surah Al-Ikhlas (3x)
arabic: قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ ﴿١﴾ اللَّهُ الصَّمَدُ ﴿٢﴾ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ ﴿٣﴾ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ ﴿٤﴾
latin: qul huwallahu ahad. allahus-samad. lam yalid wa lam yulad. wa lam yakun lahu kufuwan ahad
translation: Say: He is Allah, [who is] One. (1) Allah, the Eternal Refuge. (2) He neither begets nor is born. (3) Nor is there to Him any equivalent. (4)
notes: Recited 3 times in both morning and evening. Together with Al-Falaq and An-Nas, these three surahs are sufficient protection against all harm.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever recites Surah Al-Ikhlas, Al-Falaq, and An-Nas three times each in the morning and evening, they will suffice you against everything.' (HR. Abu Dawud No. 5082, at-Tirmidhi No. 3575, authenticated by al-Albani)

title: Morning & Evening: Reciting Surah Al-Falaq (3x)
arabic: قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ ﴿١﴾ مِن شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ ﴿٢﴾ وَمِن شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ ﴿٣﴾ وَمِن شَرِّ النَّفَّاثَاتِ فِي الْعُقَدِ ﴿٤﴾ وَمِن شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ ﴿٥﴾
latin: qul a'udhu bi rabbil-falaq. min sharri ma khalaq. wa min sharri ghasiqin idha waqab. wa min sharrin-naffathati fil-'uqad. wa min sharri hasidin idha hasad
translation: Say: I seek refuge with the Lord of the daybreak, (1) from the evil of what He has created, (2) and from the evil of darkness when it settles, (3) and from the evil of those who blow on knots, (4) and from the evil of an envier when he envies. (5)
notes: Recited 3 times in both morning and evening. Part of the three protective surahs (Al-Mu'awwidhatayn + Al-Ikhlas), recited together with Al-Ikhlas and An-Nas.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever recites Surah Al-Ikhlas, Al-Falaq, and An-Nas three times each in the morning and evening, they will suffice you against everything.' (HR. Abu Dawud No. 5082, at-Tirmidhi No. 3575, authenticated by al-Albani)

title: Morning & Evening: Reciting Surah An-Nas (3x)
arabic: قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ ﴿١﴾ مَلِكِ النَّاسِ ﴿٢﴾ إِلَهِ النَّاسِ ﴿٣﴾ مِن شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ ﴿٤﴾ الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ ﴿٥﴾ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ ﴿٦﴾
latin: qul a'udhu bi rabbin-nas. malikin-nas. ilahin-nas. min sharril-waswasil-khannas. alladhi yuwaswisu fi sudurin-nas. minal-jinnati wan-nas
translation: Say: I seek refuge with the Lord of mankind, (1) the Sovereign of mankind, (2) the God of mankind, (3) from the evil of the retreating whisperer, (4) who whispers [evil] into the breasts of mankind, (5) from among the jinn and mankind. (6)
notes: Recited 3 times in both morning and evening. Completes the three protective surahs together with Al-Ikhlas and Al-Falaq.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever recites Surah Al-Ikhlas, Al-Falaq, and An-Nas three times each in the morning and evening, they will suffice you against everything.' (HR. Abu Dawud No. 5082, at-Tirmidhi No. 3575, authenticated by al-Albani)

title: Morning: Declaration of Allah's Oneness and Power (10x)
arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
latin: la ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamdu yuhyi wa yumitu wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadir
translation: There is no god but Allah alone, with no partner. To Him belongs all sovereignty and all praise. He gives life and causes death, and He is over all things powerful.
notes: Recited 10 times in the morning only. This brief but powerful dhikr carries enormous rewards and grants full protection from Shaytan throughout the day until evening.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever says this ten times in the morning — it will be as if he freed four slaves from the descendants of Isma'il. Ten good deeds will be written for him, ten bad deeds erased, he will be raised ten levels in rank, and it will be a shield from Shaytan until evening.' (HR. Ahmad No. 16862, an-Nasa'i, authenticated by al-Albani)
source: HR. Ahmad No. 16862, an-Nasa'i (Amal al-Yawm wal-Laylah No. 24)

title: Morning & Evening: SubhanAllah wa Bihamdihi (100x)
arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ
latin: subhanallahi wa bihamdih
translation: Glory be to Allah and all praise is for Him.
notes: Recited 100 times in the morning and 100 times in the evening. This simple form is different from the longer tasbih in entry #88 (adada khalqihi... 3x) — this shorter form carries the specific reward of 100 repetitions.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever says SubhanAllah wa bihamdihi 100 times in the morning and 100 times in the evening — no one will come on the Day of Resurrection with anything better than what he brought, except someone who said the same or more.' (HR. Muslim No. 2692, Bukhari No. 6405)

title: Morning & Evening: Verses of Allah's Sovereignty (Al-Mu'minun 115-118)
arabic: أَفَحَسِبْتُمْ أَنَّمَا خَلَقْنَاكُمْ عَبَثًا وَأَنَّكُمْ إِلَيْنَا لَا تُرْجَعُونَ ﴿١١٥﴾ فَتَعَالَى اللَّهُ الْمَلِكُ الْحَقُّ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْكَرِيمِ ﴿١١٦﴾ وَمَن يَدْعُ مَعَ اللَّهِ إِلَهًا آخَرَ لَا بُرْهَانَ لَهُ بِهِ فَإِنَّمَا حِسَابُهُ عِندَ رَبِّهِ إِنَّهُ لَا يُفْلِحُ الْكَافِرُونَ ﴿١١٧﴾ وَقُل رَّبِّ اغْفِرْ وَارْحَمْ وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ الرَّاحِمِينَ ﴿١١٨﴾
latin: afahasibtum annamā khalaqnākum 'abathan wa annakum ilaynā lā turja'ūn. fata'ālallāhul-malikul-haqq, lā ilāha illā huwa rabbul-'arshil-karīm. wa man yad'u ma'allāhi ilāhan ākhara lā burhāna lahu bih, fa'innamā hisābuhu 'inda rabbih, innahu lā yufliḥul-kāfirūn. wa qul rabbigh-fir warḥam wa anta khayrur-rāḥimīn
translation: Did you think that We created you without purpose and that you would not be returned to Us? (115) So exalted is Allah, the Sovereign, the Truth; there is no deity except Him, Lord of the Honorable Throne. (116) And whoever invokes besides Allah another deity — for which he has no proof — his account is only with his Lord. Indeed, the disbelievers will not succeed. (117) And say: My Lord, forgive and have mercy, for You are the Best of those who show mercy. (118)
notes: These four verses are recited in the morning and evening. The concluding supplication (verse 118) — 'My Lord, forgive and have mercy' — is one of the most concise and comprehensive prayers in the Quran.
benefits: Reciting these verses affirms the purposefulness of creation and Allah's absolute sovereignty, and ends with a direct supplication for forgiveness and mercy.

title: Morning, Evening & Before Sleeping: Seeking Refuge in Allah (Allahumma Fatiras-Samawat)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ فَاطِرَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأَرْضِ، عَالِمَ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ، رَبَّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَمَلِيكَهُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ نَفْسِي، وَشَرِّ الشَّيْطَانِ وَشِرْكِهِ
latin: allahumma fatiras-samawati wal-ard, 'alimal-ghaybi wash-shahadah, rabba kulli shay'in wa malikah, ash-hadu an la ilaha illa ant, a'udhu bika min sharri nafsi, wa sharrish-shaytani wa shirkihi
translation: O Allah, Creator of the heavens and the earth, Knower of the unseen and the seen, Lord and Sovereign of everything — I bear witness that there is no god but You. I seek refuge in You from the evil of my own soul and from the evil of Shaytan and his associates.
notes: Recited in the morning, in the evening, and before sleeping. This dua encompasses both tawhid (declaration of Allah's oneness) and isti'adhah (seeking refuge), making it a comprehensive spiritual shield.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ instructed 'Ali ibn Abi Talib (may Allah be pleased with him) to recite this dua every morning, every evening, and before sleeping. (HR. Abu Dawud No. 5067, at-Tirmidhi No. 3392, authenticated by al-Albani)
source: HR. Abu Dawud No. 5067, at-Tirmidhi No. 3392

title: Before Sleeping: Reciting Surah Al-Kafirun (Declaration of Disavowal from Shirk)
arabic: قُلْ يَا أَيُّهَا الْكَافِرُونَ ﴿١﴾ لَا أَعْبُدُ مَا تَعْبُدُونَ ﴿٢﴾ وَلَا أَنتُمْ عَابِدُونَ مَا أَعْبُدُ ﴿٣﴾ وَلَا أَنَا عَابِدٌ مَّا عَبَدتُّمْ ﴿٤﴾ وَلَا أَنتُمْ عَابِدُونَ مَا أَعْبُدُ ﴿٥﴾ لَكُمْ دِينُكُمْ وَلِيَ دِينِ ﴿٦﴾
latin: qul ya ayyuhal-kafirun. la a'budu ma ta'budun. wa la antum 'abiduna ma a'bud. wa la ana 'abidun ma 'abadtum. wa la antum 'abiduna ma a'bud. lakum dinukum wa liya din
translation: Say: O disbelievers, (1) I do not worship what you worship. (2) Nor are you worshippers of what I worship. (3) Nor will I be a worshipper of what you worship. (4) Nor will you be worshippers of what I worship. (5) For you is your religion, and for me is my religion. (6)
notes: Recited once before sleeping. This surah is known as a 'bara'ah min ash-shirk' — a declaration of complete disavowal from polytheism — and protects the reciter throughout the night.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said to Nawfal al-Ashja'i: 'Recite Surah Al-Kafirun, then sleep after it — for it is a declaration of disavowal from shirk.' (HR. Abu Dawud No. 5055, at-Tirmidhi No. 3403, authenticated by al-Albani)
source: QS. Al-Kafirun: 1-6, HR. Abu Dawud No. 5055

title: Morning & Evening: Closing Tasbih (SubhanAllah 33x, Alhamdulillah 33x, Allahu Akbar 33x + Tahlil)
arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللهِ (٣٣×)، الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ (٣٣×)، اللهُ أَكْبَرُ (٣٣×)، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
latin: subhanallah (33x), alhamdulillah (33x), allahu akbar (33x), la ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamdu wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadir
translation: Glory be to Allah (33x). All praise is for Allah (33x). Allah is the Greatest (33x). There is no god but Allah alone, with no partner — to Him belongs all sovereignty and all praise, and He is over all things powerful.
notes: Recited in the morning and evening as a closing dhikr. The total count reaches 100 with the final tahlil. Each phrase is said 33 times, then completed by the declaration of Allah's oneness and power.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever glorifies Allah (SubhanAllah) 33 times after every prayer, praises Him (Alhamdulillah) 33 times, and magnifies Him (Allahu Akbar) 33 times — and completes 100 with: La ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lah... — his sins will be forgiven even if they were like the foam of the sea.' (HR. Muslim No. 597, Abu Dawud No. 1504). Also recited as a morning/evening closing adhkar.

title: Morning & Evening: Opening Verses of Surah Al-Baqarah (1-5)
arabic: الم ﴿١﴾ ذَٰلِكَ الْكِتَابُ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ هُدًى لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ ﴿٢﴾ الَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْغَيْبِ وَيُقِيمُونَ الصَّلَاةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَاهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ ﴿٣﴾ وَالَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَمَا أُنزِلَ مِن قَبْلِكَ وَبِالْآخِرَةِ هُمْ يُوقِنُونَ ﴿٤﴾ أُوْلَٰئِكَ عَلَىٰ هُدًى مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَأُوْلَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ ﴿٥﴾
latin: alif lām mīm. dhālikal-kitābu lā rayba fīh, hudal-lil-muttaqīn. alladhīna yu'minūna bil-ghaybi wa yuqīmūnas-salāta wa mimmā razaqnāhum yunfiqūn. walladhīna yu'minūna bimā unzila ilayka wa mā unzila min qablika wa bil-ākhirati hum yūqinūn. ulā'ika 'alā hudam mir-rabbihim wa ulā'ika humul-muflihūn
translation: Alif Lam Mim. (1) This is the Book in which there is no doubt, a guidance for the God-fearing — (2) those who believe in the unseen, establish prayer, and spend out of what We have provided for them, (3) and those who believe in what has been revealed to you, and what was revealed before you, and of the Hereafter they are certain. (4) Those are upon guidance from their Lord, and those are the successful. (5)
notes: Recited in the morning and evening. Together with Ayat al-Kursi (255-257), Al-Baqarah 284-286, and other key passages, these opening verses form a complete morning/evening Quranic recitation program that provides comprehensive spiritual protection and guidance.
benefits: Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that whoever recites the first four verses of Surah Al-Baqarah along with Ayat al-Kursi (255-257) and the last three verses of Surah Al-Baqarah (284-286) — Shaytan will not come near him or his family that day, and no calamity will befall him. (Mentioned by Ibn Kathir and reported in at-Tabarani and ad-Darimi)

title: Morning & Evening: Reaffirming the Fitrah of Islam (Asbahna 'ala Fitratil-Islam)
arabic: أَصْبَحْنَا عَلَى فِطْرَةِ الْإِسْلَامِ، وَعَلَى كَلِمَةِ الْإِخْلَاصِ، وَعَلَى دِينِ نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، وَعَلَى مِلَّةِ أَبِينَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا مُسْلِمًا وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
latin: asbahna 'ala fitratil-islam, wa 'ala kalimati'l-ikhlas, wa 'ala dini nabiyyina muhammadin sallallahu 'alayhi wa sallam, wa 'ala millati abina ibrahima hanifan musliman wa ma kana minal-mushrikin
translation: We have reached the morning upon the fitrah (natural disposition) of Islam, upon the word of sincerity [La ilaha illallah], upon the religion of our Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, and upon the way of our forefather Ibrahim — a true Muslim who was not among the polytheists.
notes: Morning: أَصْبَحْنَا (asbahna) | Evening: أَمْسَيْنَا (amsayna) — change only the first word. This dhikr reaffirms one's complete commitment to Islam, tawhid, the Prophetic way, and the Abrahamic tradition with every morning and evening.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever says this in the morning has indeed offered his due thanks for that day; and whoever says it in the evening has offered his due thanks for that night.' (HR. Ahmad Vol. 3/406, an-Nasa'i — Amal al-Yawm wal-Laylah No. 34, Ibn al-Sunni No. 343)
source: HR. Ahmad Vol. 3/406, an-Nasa'i (Amal al-Yawm wal-Laylah) No. 34

title: Morning, Evening & Before Sleeping: Dua of Abu Bakr — Seeking Refuge from Evil of the Soul
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ عَالِمَ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ، فَاطِرَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ، رَبَّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَمَلِيكَهُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ نَفْسِي وَمِنْ شَرِّ الشَّيْطَانِ وَشِرْكِهِ، وَأَنْ أَقْتَرِفَ عَلَى نَفْسِي سُوءًا أَوْ أَجُرَّهُ إِلَى مُسْلِمٍ
latin: allahumma 'alimal-ghaybi wash-shahadah, fatiras-samawati wal-ard, rabba kulli shay'in wa malikah, ash-hadu an la ilaha illa ant, a'udhu bika min sharri nafsi wa min sharris-shaytani wa shirkihi, wa an aqtarifa 'ala nafsi su'an aw ajurrahu ila muslim
translation: O Allah, Knower of the unseen and the seen, Creator of the heavens and the earth, Lord and Master of all things — I bear witness that there is no god but You. I seek refuge in You from the evil of my own soul, from the evil of Satan and his polytheism, and from committing evil against myself or bringing it upon a Muslim.
notes: Recited in the morning, in the evening, and before sleeping. This dua is distinct from the shorter version (Entry 115) by its additional clause — seeking refuge not only from one's own soul and Satan but specifically from wronging oneself or inflicting harm upon any Muslim.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said to Abu Bakr as-Siddiq (may Allah be pleased with him): 'Say this in the morning, in the evening, and when you go to bed.' (HR. Abu Dawud No. 5084, at-Tirmidhi No. 3529, authenticated by al-Albani)
source: HR. Abu Dawud No. 5084, at-Tirmidhi No. 3529

title: Morning & Evening: Complete Trust in Allah's Will and Power
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّي لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، عَلَيْكَ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَأَنْتَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ، مَا شَاءَ اللهُ كَانَ وَمَا لَمْ يَشَأْ لَمْ يَكُنْ، لَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللهِ الْعَلِيِّ الْعَظِيمِ، أَعْلَمُ أَنَّ اللهَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ وَأَنَّ اللهَ قَدْ أَحَاطَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عِلْمًا، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ نَفْسِي وَمِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ دَابَّةٍ أَنْتَ آخِذٌ بِنَاصِيَتِهَا، إِنَّ رَبِّي عَلَى صِرَاطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ
latin: allahumma anta rabbi la ilaha illa ant, 'alayka tawakkaltu wa anta rabbul-'arshil-'azim, ma sha'allahu kana wa ma lam yasha' lam yakun, la hawla wa la quwwata illa billahil-'aliyyil-'azim, a'lamu annallaha 'ala kulli shay'in qadir wa annallaha qad ahata bikulli shay'in 'ilma. allahumma inni a'udhu bika min sharri nafsi wa min sharri kulli dabbatin anta akhidhun binasiyatiha, inna rabbi 'ala siratin mustaqim
translation: O Allah, You are my Lord, there is no god but You. In You I place my trust, and You are the Lord of the Magnificent Throne. Whatever Allah wills happens, and what He does not will does not happen. There is no power and no strength except with Allah, the Most High, the Most Great. I know that Allah has power over all things and that His knowledge encompasses all things. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the evil of my own soul and from the evil of every creature that You hold by its forelock. Indeed, my Lord is on a straight path.
notes: A comprehensive morning and evening supplication combining absolute trust (tawakkul), acknowledgment of Allah's will (qadar), declaration of no power except Allah (la hawla), and seeking refuge from all harm — making it one of the most complete protective formulas in the Sunnah.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said to a man: 'Say in the morning and in the evening: [this dua]. For whoever says it will be protected from everything he fears.' (HR. al-Bukhari No. 6851, Abu Dawud No. 5083, Ahmad)
source: HR. al-Bukhari No. 6851, Abu Dawud No. 5083

title: Morning: La Ilaha Illallah Wahdahu (100x) — Greatest Daily Dhikr
arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
latin: la ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamd, wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadir
translation: There is no god but Allah alone, with no partner. His is the sovereignty and His is all praise, and He has power over all things.
notes: Recited 100 times daily (preferably in the morning). This version does NOT include 'yuhyi wa yumit' — it is distinct from the ×10 morning variant (Entry 112) which includes that phrase. The ×100 count carries its own specific reward mentioned in Muslim 2691.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever says this 100 times a day will have the reward of freeing 10 slaves. 100 good deeds will be written for him, 100 bad deeds will be erased, it will be a shield against Satan until the evening, and no one will come on the Day of Resurrection with better deeds — except one who has done the same or more.' (HR. Muslim No. 2691, Abu Dawud No. 5091, at-Tirmidhi No. 3469)
source: HR. Muslim No. 2691, Abu Dawud No. 5091, at-Tirmidhi No. 3469

title: After Each Obligatory Prayer: Closing Glorification — Verses of Al-Saffat (180-182)
arabic: سُبْحَانَ رَبِّكَ رَبِّ الْعِزَّةِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ ﴿١٨٠﴾ وَسَلَامٌ عَلَى الْمُرْسَلِينَ ﴿١٨١﴾ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ ﴿١٨٢﴾
latin: subhana rabbika rabbil-'izzati 'amma yasifun, wa salamun 'alal-mursalin, wal-hamdulillahi rabbil-'alamin
translation: Glory be to your Lord, the Lord of Honour, far above what they attribute to Him. (180) And peace be upon the Messengers. (181) And all praise belongs to Allah, Lord of all the worlds. (182)
notes: Recited after each obligatory (fardh) prayer. These three closing verses of Surah As-Saffat combine glorification of Allah, peace upon all Prophets, and praise — making them a complete seal for each prayer.
benefits: Ubayy ibn Ka'b (may Allah be pleased with him) narrated that the Prophet ﷺ used to recite these three verses at the end of his prayer (after the obligatory prayers). (HR. Abu Dawud No. 454, at-Tirmidhi No. 363, Ibn Majah No. 928)
source: QS. As-Saffat: 180-182, HR. Abu Dawud No. 454

title: Du'a al-Qunut: Supplication for Guidance and Well-Being (Recited in the Witr Prayer)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اهْدِنِي فِيمَنْ هَدَيْتَ، وَعَافِنِي فِيمَنْ عَافَيْتَ، وَتَوَلَّنِي فِيمَنْ تَوَلَّيْتَ، وَبَارِكْ لِي فِيمَا أَعْطَيْتَ، وَقِنِي شَرَّ مَا قَضَيْتَ، فَإِنَّكَ تَقْضِي وَلَا يُقْضَى عَلَيْكَ، وَإِنَّهُ لَا يَذِلُّ مَنْ وَالَيْتَ، وَلَا يَعِزُّ مَنْ عَادَيْتَ، تَبَارَكْتَ رَبَّنَا وَتَعَالَيْتَ
latin: allahumma ihdini fiman hadayt, wa 'afini fiman 'afayt, wa tawallani fiman tawallayt, wa barik li fima a'tayt, wa qini sharra ma qadayt, fa innaka taqdi wa la yuqda 'alayk, wa innahu la yadhillu man walayt, wa la ya'izzu man 'adayt, tabarakta rabbana wa ta'alayt
translation: O Allah, guide me among those You have guided, and grant me well-being among those You have granted well-being. Take me under Your care among those You have taken care of. Bless for me what You have given me, and protect me from the evil of what You have decreed. For indeed You decree and nothing is decreed over You. Surely whoever You befriend is not humiliated, and whoever You oppose is not exalted. Blessed are You, our Lord, and Most High.
notes: Taught by the Prophet ﷺ to Hasan ibn Ali (may Allah be pleased with them both) — recited in the Qunut of the Witr prayer.
benefits: Al-Hasan ibn Ali said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ taught me these words to say in the Qunut of the Witr prayer.' This supplication is a comprehensive plea covering five dimensions: guidance, well-being, divine guardianship, blessing in what one has been given, and protection from harmful decree. It concludes with acknowledgment of Allah's absolute sovereignty.
source: HR. Abu Dawud No. 570, Ibn Majah No. 48, at-Tirmidhi No. 3107

title: Kaffarah al-Majlis: Expiation of a Gathering
arabic: سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ وَبِحَمْدِكَ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، أَسْتَغْفِرُكَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْكَ
latin: subhanakallahumma wa bihamdika, ash-hadu an la ilaha illa anta, astaghfiruka wa atubu ilayk
translation: Glory be to You, O Allah, and all praise. I bear witness that there is no god but You. I seek Your forgiveness and repent to You.
notes: Recited when rising from any gathering or sitting — it expiates any inadvertent sins committed during the gathering.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever sits in a gathering in which much noise is made, and says before rising: Subhanakallahumma wa bihamdika, ash-hadu an la ilaha illa anta, astaghfiruka wa atubu ilayk — Allah will forgive him for what occurred in that gathering.' (HR. at-Tirmidhi No. 3433) It is a seal of remembrance and repentance that purifies one's gathering.
source: HR. Abu Dawud No. 5070, at-Tirmidhi No. 3453, an-Nasa'i No. 5524

title: Salawat Ibrahimiyyah: Sending Blessings upon the Prophet and His Family (After Each Obligatory Prayer)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَعَلَى آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ، كَمَا صَلَّيْتَ عَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَعَلَى آلِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، إِنَّكَ حَمِيدٌ مَجِيدٌ، اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَعَلَى آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ، كَمَا بَارَكْتَ عَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَعَلَى آلِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، إِنَّكَ حَمِيدٌ مَجِيدٌ
latin: allahumma salli 'ala muhammadin wa 'ala ali muhammad, kama sallayta 'ala ibrahima wa 'ala ali ibrahim, innaka hamidun majid, allahumma barik 'ala muhammadin wa 'ala ali muhammad, kama barakta 'ala ibrahima wa 'ala ali ibrahim, innaka hamidun majid
translation: O Allah, send prayers upon Muhammad and upon the family of Muhammad, just as You sent prayers upon Ibrahim and upon the family of Ibrahim. Indeed, You are the Most Praiseworthy, the Most Glorious. O Allah, send blessings upon Muhammad and upon the family of Muhammad, just as You sent blessings upon Ibrahim and upon the family of Ibrahim. Indeed, You are the Most Praiseworthy, the Most Glorious.
notes: The complete Salawat Ibrahimiyyah — the full form of sending blessings upon the Prophet ﷺ, recited in the final tashahhud of every prayer.
benefits: The companions asked: 'O Messenger of Allah, how should we send prayers upon you?' The Prophet ﷺ replied with this formula. Sending salawat upon the Prophet ﷺ is among the greatest acts of worship — the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever sends one prayer upon me, Allah sends ten prayers upon him, erases ten of his sins, and raises him ten degrees.' (HR. an-Nasa'i No. 1297)
source: HR. Ibn Majah No. 1517, at-Tirmidhi No. 3648, Ibn Khuzaima Vol. 1/511

title: Supplication When Drinking Water
arabic: وَسَقَاهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ شَرَابًا طَهُورًا
latin: wa saqahum rabbuhum sharaban tahura
translation: And their Lord will give them a pure drink.
notes: Recited with gratitude when drinking water, as a reminder of the pure drink of Paradise promised by Allah to the righteous.
benefits: This verse from Surah Al-Insan describes one of the rewards of Paradise. Reciting it when drinking water connects a simple daily act of refreshment to the ultimate eternal reward, transforming it into a moment of worship and longing for Allah's promise.

title: Supplication Before Eating (Seeking Barakah in Food)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِيمَا رَزَقْتَنَا وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
latin: allahumma barik lana fima razaqtana wa qina 'adhaban-nar
translation: O Allah, bless for us what You have provided us and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.
notes: An additional pre-meal supplication recited alongside 'Bismillah.' Combines gratitude for food with a fundamental prayer for protection from Hellfire.
benefits: This dua pairs the blessing of sustenance with a supplication for the Hereafter, teaching that every meal should be a moment of both gratitude and remembrance of Allah's mercy and warning.

title: Supplication After Eating (Short Gratitude Version with 'Muslims')
arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَطْعَمَنَا وَسَقَانَا وَجَعَلَنَا مُسْلِمِينَ
latin: alhamdulillahilladhi at'amana wa saqana wa ja'alana muslimin
translation: All praise is to Allah who has fed us, given us to drink, and made us Muslims.
notes: A concise post-meal supplication that thanks Allah for three blessings at once: food, drink, and Islam. Taught widely in Islamic schools as the post-meal dua.
benefits: This short dua encompasses three of the greatest daily blessings from Allah — sustenance, water, and the gift of Islam itself. Acknowledging all three after every meal cultivates deep and consistent gratitude.

title: Supplication Upon Entering the House (Asking for Good Entry and Exit)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ الْمَوْلِجِ وَخَيْرَ الْمَخْرَجِ، بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَلَجْنَا وَبِسْمِ اللَّهِ خَرَجْنَا، وَعَلَى اللَّهِ رَبِّنَا تَوَكَّلْنَا
latin: allahumma inni as'aluka khayral-mawliji wa khayral-makhraj, bismillahi walajna wa bismillahi kharajna, wa 'alallahi rabbina tawakkalna
translation: O Allah, I ask You for the best entry and the best exit. In the name of Allah we enter, in the name of Allah we leave, and in Allah our Lord we place our trust.
notes: The complete supplication recited upon entering one's home; distinct from the Salam greeting versions. Combines a request for goodness in all coming and going with bismillah and complete tawakkul.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'When a man enters his house and mentions the name of Allah upon entering and upon eating, Satan says: There is no place to sleep and no dinner for you here.' This dua fortifies the home as a sanctuary of tawakkul and divine blessing.

title: Jazakallahu Khayran: Expressing Gratitude to Someone
arabic: جَزَاكَ اللَّهُ خَيْرًا
latin: jazakallahu khayran
translation: May Allah reward you with goodness.
notes: Said to someone who has done a favor, shown kindness, or helped in any way. Plural form: 'Jazakumullahu khayran' (for a group).
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever has a good deed done to him and says Jazakallahu khayran has fully expressed his gratitude.' It is considered more complete than any human thanks, because it delegates infinite reward to Allah — the only One who can truly repay a good deed.
source: HR. at-Tirmidhi No. 2035, authenticated by al-Albani

title: Supplication Before Studying (For Knowledge and Understanding)
arabic: رَبِّ زِدْنِي عِلْمًا وَارْزُقْنِي فَهْمًا
latin: rabbi zidni 'ilman warzuqni fahma
translation: My Lord, increase me in knowledge and grant me understanding.
notes: Recited before studying, attending a lesson, or seeking any knowledge. The first phrase is directly from QS. Ta-Ha: 114.
benefits: The Quran is the only place where Allah commands us to ask for more of something — and that something is knowledge ('Rabbi zidni ilma' — QS. Ta-Ha: 114). Adding the prayer for understanding (fahm) transforms knowledge into wisdom and beneficial action.
source: QS. Ta-Ha: 114 (first phrase); traditional Islamic educational dua

title: Supplication Before Reading the Quran (Opening Wisdom and Mercy)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ افْتَحْ عَلَيْنَا حِكْمَتَكَ وَانْشُرْ عَلَيْنَا مِنْ خَزَائِنِ رَحْمَتِكَ يَا أَرْحَمَ الرَّاحِمِينَ
latin: allahumma iftah 'alayna hikmataka wanshur 'alayna min khaza'ini rahmatika ya arhamar-rahimin
translation: O Allah, open Your wisdom upon us and spread upon us from the treasuries of Your mercy, O Most Merciful of the merciful.
notes: Recited before beginning a Quran recitation or Islamic study session.
benefits: True understanding of the Quran comes only through divine guidance. This dua asks Allah to unlock wisdom and shower boundless mercy upon the reader before they begin — an act of humility acknowledging that only Allah can reveal the depths of His Book.

title: Supplication After Reading the Quran (Comprehensive Post-Recitation Dua)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ ارْحَمْنَا بِالْقُرْآنِ، وَاجْعَلْهُ لَنَا إِمَامًا وَنُورًا وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةً، اللَّهُمَّ ذَكِّرْنَا مِنْهُ مَا نَسِينَا، وَعَلِّمْنَا مِنْهُ مَا جَهِلْنَا، وَارْزُقْنَا تِلَاوَتَهُ آنَاءَ اللَّيْلِ وَأَطْرَافَ النَّهَارِ، وَاجْعَلْهُ لَنَا حُجَّةً يَا رَبَّ الْعَالَمِينَ
latin: allahumma irhamna bil-quran, waj'alhu lana imaman wa nuran wa hudan wa rahmah, allahumma dhakkirna minhu ma nasina, wa 'allimna minhu ma jahilna, warzuqna tilawatahu ana'al-layli wa atrafa'n-nahar, waj'alhu lana hujjatan ya rabbal-'alamin
translation: O Allah, have mercy on us through the Quran, and make it for us a guide, a light, a guidance and a mercy. O Allah, remind us of what we have forgotten of it, teach us what we are ignorant of it, provide us with its recitation in the depths of the night and the edges of the day, and make it a proof for us, O Lord of the worlds.
notes: Recited after completing a Quran recitation or study session.
benefits: This dua makes five comprehensive requests: mercy through the Quran, making the Quran a guide and light, recalling what was forgotten, learning what is unknown, consistency in recitation, and the Quran as an intercessor on the Day of Judgment — a perfect seal for any Quran session.

title: Supplication of Gratitude Upon Receiving Any Blessing
arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
latin: alhamdulillahi rabbil-'alamin
translation: All praise is to Allah, Lord of all the worlds.
notes: Recited upon receiving any blessing, good news, or positive event as an expression of complete gratitude to Allah.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'The best dhikr is La ilaha illallah, and the best supplication is Alhamdulillah.' This phrase — the second verse of Al-Fatiha, recited seventeen times daily in prayer — is the most complete declaration that all good comes from Allah alone.
source: QS. Al-Fatiha: 2; HR. at-Tirmidhi No. 3383, Ibn Majah No. 3800

title: Supplication When Undressing or Removing Clothes
arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الَّذِي لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ
latin: bismillahilladhi la ilaha illa huw
translation: In the name of Allah, besides whom there is no deity.
notes: Recited when removing clothes, especially before bathing or changing. Provides spiritual protection during the moment of undressing.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'The barrier between the eyes of jinn and the nakedness of the children of Adam — when one removes their garment — is to say Bismillah.' This extended form adds the declaration of Allah's unique divinity for additional protection.
source: HR. at-Tirmidhi No. 606; Islamic educational tradition

title: Prayer for Parents (Forgiveness and Mercy)
arabic: رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِوَالِدَيَّ وَارْحَمْهُمَا كَمَا رَبَّيَانِي صَغِيرًا
latin: rabbighfir li wa liwalidayya warhamhuma kama rabbayani saghira
translation: My Lord, forgive me and my parents, and have mercy on them as they raised me when I was small.
notes: Recited regularly as a supplication for one's parents, especially while they are alive.
benefits: From QS. Al-Isra: 24, where Allah commands children to treat their parents with humility and to supplicate for them. The connection between the care a child received when small and the mercy requested for parents makes this one of the most moving duas in Islam.

title: Prayer for a Righteous Child (Dua of Prophet Ibrahim)
arabic: رَبِّ هَبْ لِي مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ
latin: rabbi hab li minas-salihin
translation: My Lord, grant me a child from the righteous.
notes: The prayer of Prophet Ibrahim ﻋﻠﻴﻪ ﺍﻟﺴﻼﻡ asking for a pious offspring. Recited by those seeking children or praying for their children to be righteous.
benefits: Allah answered this prayer with the birth of Prophet Ismail ﻋﻠﻴﻪ ﺍﻟﺴﻼﻡ — one of the greatest prophets. This is the most concise and powerful supplication for righteous children, connected directly to the lineage of prophethood.

title: Supplication Upon Hearing Thunder or Seeing Lightning
arabic: سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي يُسَبِّحُ الرَّعْدُ بِحَمْدِهِ وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ مِنْ خِيفَتِهِ
latin: subhanalladhi yusabbihur-ra'du bihamdihi wal-mala'ikatu min khifatih
translation: Glory be to Him whom the thunder glorifies with His praise, and the angels, out of awe of Him.
notes: Recited when thunder is heard or lightning is seen.
benefits: From QS. Ar-Ra'd: 13. When the believer joins the thunder and the angels in glorifying Allah during a storm, they connect to the universal worship of creation — transforming fear of the storm into spiritual awe and closeness to Allah.

title: Supplication After Hearing the Iqamah (Before Each Obligatory Prayer)
arabic: أَقَامَهَا اللَّهُ وَأَدَامَهَا، اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْنِي مِنْ صَالِحِي أَهْلِهَا
latin: aqamahallahu wa adamaha, allahumma ij'alni min salihi ahliha
translation: May Allah establish it and perpetuate it. O Allah, make me among the righteous people of the prayer.
notes: Recited when the iqamah (the call announcing the prayer is beginning) is heard.
benefits: The iqamah is the call to rise and stand for prayer. By asking to be among the 'righteous people of the prayer,' the worshipper sets the highest intention before even beginning — asking Allah not just to stand physically, but to stand among those whose prayers are truly accepted.
source: HR. Abu Dawud No. 528, authenticated by al-Albani

title: Supplication for Living, Dying, and Being Resurrected with Iman
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَحْيِنَا بِالْإِيمَانِ، اللَّهُمَّ أَمِتْنَا بِالْإِيمَانِ، اللَّهُمَّ احْشُرْنَا بِالْإِيمَانِ وَأَدْخِلْنَا الْجَنَّةَ مَعَ الْإِيمَانِ
latin: allahumma ahyina bil-iman, allahumma amitna bil-iman, allahumma ahshurna bil-iman wa adkhilna al-jannata ma'al-iman
translation: O Allah, let us live with faith. O Allah, let us die with faith. O Allah, gather us with faith and admit us into Paradise with faith.
notes: A comprehensive supplication asking that iman (faith) permeate every stage of one's existence: life, death, resurrection, and Paradise.
benefits: The greatest concern for every Muslim is to die and be resurrected upon iman. This dua asks Allah for that protection at every stage, recognizing that iman is not merely a possession but a state that must be constantly preserved by Allah's grace.

title: Prayer for Protection of One's Family from Corruption
arabic: رَبِّ نَجِّنِي وَأَهْلِي مِمَّا يَعْمَلُونَ
latin: rabbi najjini wa ahli mimma ya'malun
translation: My Lord, save me and my family from what they do.
notes: The prayer of Prophet Lut ﻋﻠﻴﻪ ﺍﻟﺴﻼﻡ asking Allah to protect his family from the corruption of the surrounding people.
benefits: This dua teaches that a Muslim's first responsibility is the spiritual safety of their household. When the environment becomes corrupt, we turn to Allah — the only One who can truly rescue our families from moral harm.

title: Supplication Upon Seeing a Deceased Person
arabic: سُبْحَانَ الْحَيِّ الَّذِي لَا يَمُوتُ، اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِهَذَا الْمَيِّتِ وَارْحَمْهُ وَآنِسْ فِي الْقَبْرِ وَحْدَتَهُ وَغُرْبَتَهُ وَنَوِّرْ قَبْرَهُ
latin: subhanal-hayyilladhi la yamut, allahumma ighfir lihadhal-mayyiti warhamhu wa anis fil-qabri wahdatahu wa ghurbatahu wa nawwir qabrahu
translation: Glory be to the Ever-Living who does not die. O Allah, forgive this deceased, have mercy on him, keep him company in his loneliness and isolation in the grave, and illuminate his grave.
notes: Recited when one encounters a deceased person or sees a corpse. Use 'لِهَذِهِ الْمَيِّتَةِ' and feminine pronouns for a female.
benefits: Encountering death is a powerful reminder of the Hereafter. This supplication turns the moment into an act of mercy for the deceased and a spiritual reminder for the living. The opening phrase — glorifying the Ever-Living — is a profound declaration that only Allah is eternal.
source: HR. al-Hakim, Ibn al-Sunni (Amal al-Yawm wal-Laylah)

title: Supplication After a Good Dream
arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ
latin: alhamdulillah
translation: All praise is for Allah.
notes: Recited immediately upon waking from a good dream. The full Sunnah: praise Allah, then share the dream only with someone loved or trusted — not with those who may be envious.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Good dreams are from Allah. If one of you sees something he likes, let him not tell it except to one he loves.' And: 'A good dream is one of the forty-six parts of prophethood.' Praising Allah upon a good dream is an act of gratitude for this divine gift.

title: Supplication Upon Having a Nightmare or Bad Dream
arabic: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ
latin: a'udhu billahi minash-shaytanir-rajim
translation: I seek refuge in Allah from the accursed Satan.
notes: Recited immediately upon waking from a nightmare. Full Sunnah: recite this, spit gently to the left three times, turn to the other side, and do not tell anyone the contents of the bad dream. One may also rise and pray two rak'ahs.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'If one of you sees something he dislikes, let him spit three times to his left, seek refuge from Satan three times, and turn from the side he was on — for it will not harm him.' Bad dreams are from Satan, and this supplication with the accompanying actions dispels his influence.

title: Supplication for Protecting Children from Shaytan and the Evil Eye
arabic: أُعِيذُكَ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّةِ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْطَانٍ وَهَامَّةٍ وَمِنْ كُلِّ عَيْنٍ لَامَّةٍ
latin: u'idhuka bi kalimatillahit-tammati min kulli shaytanin wa hammatin wa min kulli 'aynin lammah
translation: I seek protection for you in the perfect words of Allah from every devil, every harmful creature, and from every envious evil eye.
notes: Recited by parents or guardians over children as protection. 'Ka' (you, male) means this dua is said FOR a child. Use 'ki' (كِ) for a female child.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ used these exact words to protect his grandsons Hasan and Husayn, saying: 'Your forefather Ibrahim used to seek protection for Ismail and Ishaq with these words.' (HR. al-Bukhari No. 3371) — connecting this prophetic protection directly to the lineage of the prophets.
source: HR. al-Bukhari No. 3371, Abu Dawud No. 4737

title: Supplication Upon Entering the Graveyard
arabic: السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ دَارَ قَوْمٍ مُؤْمِنِينَ وَإِنَّا إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ بِكُمْ لَاحِقُونَ
latin: as-salamu 'alaykum dara qawmin mu'minina wa inna in sha'allahu bikum lahiqun
translation: Peace be upon you, O abode of a believing people. Indeed, we will, if Allah wills, join you.
notes: Recited upon entering the graveyard (maqbarah). The greeting of the living to the dead, as taught by the Prophet ﷺ.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ taught this greeting for visiting the graves. It acknowledges the deep brotherhood between the living and the dead in faith, and the inevitability of one's own death — transforming a visit to the graves into a profound spiritual reflection on the Hereafter.
source: HR. Muslim No. 975, Ibn Majah No. 1547

title: Supplication for a Deceased Person (Concise Form)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لَهُ وَارْحَمْهُ وَعَافِهِ وَاعْفُ عَنْهُ
latin: allahumma ighfir lahu warhamhu wa 'afihi wa'fu 'anh
translation: O Allah, forgive him, have mercy on him, grant him well-being, and pardon him.
notes: A short but comprehensive dua for any deceased Muslim. Use feminine pronouns ('laha', 'warhamha', 'wa'afiha', 'wa'fu anha') for a female. Can be made at any time, not only at the funeral.
benefits: This dua covers the four most essential needs of the deceased: forgiveness of sins, divine mercy, spiritual well-being, and complete pardon. The Prophet ﷺ himself prayed for the deceased with similar words. It is among the most beloved duas one can make for those who have passed.
source: HR. Muslim No. 963, Abu Dawud No. 3201

title: Supplication for Healing the Sick — The Ruqyah of the Prophet ﷺ
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ النَّاسِ أَذْهِبِ الْبَاسَ، اشْفِ أَنْتَ الشَّافِي، لَا شِفَاءَ إِلَّا شِفَاؤُكَ شِفَاءً لَا يُغَادِرُ سَقَمًا
latin: allahumma rabban-nasi adhhib il-ba's, ishfi antal-shafi, la shifa'a illa shifa'uk, shifa'an la yughadiru saqama
translation: O Allah, Lord of mankind, remove the illness. Heal — for You are the Healer. There is no cure except Your cure, a cure that leaves no illness.
notes: Recited while placing the hand on the place of pain or illness. This is the prophetic ruqyah recited by or for the sick person — distinct from the visiting-the-sick supplication which is recited by the visitor.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ used to recite this over those who were ill, placing his hand on the place of pain. It declares Allah as the sole Healer — above all medicine — and asks for a complete cure with no trace of illness remaining.

title: Supplication for Protection from Four Major Harms (Recited After Each Obligatory Prayer)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابِ جَهَنَّمَ، وَمِنْ عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ، وَمِنْ فِتْنَةِ الْمَحْيَا وَالْمَمَاتِ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ فِتْنَةِ الْمَسِيحِ الدَّجَّالِ
latin: allahumma inni a'udhu bika min 'adhabi jahannam, wa min 'adhabil-qabr, wa min fitnatil-mahya wal-mamat, wa min sharri fitnatil-masihid-dajjal
translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the punishment of Hell, from the punishment of the grave, from the trials of life and death, and from the evil trial of the False Messiah (Dajjal).
notes: Recited in the final tashahhud (after At-Tahiyyat and Salawat Ibrahimiyyah) before the taslim. One of the most important duas of the prayer, as commanded by the Prophet ﷺ.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ commanded: 'When one of you completes the tashahhud, let him seek refuge in Allah from four things' — and mentioned these four. This dua protects against the four greatest trials: punishment of Hell, punishment of the grave, trials in life and at death, and the trial of the Dajjal.

title: Supplication for Protection from Calamity, Enemy Gloating, and Bad Destiny
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ دَرَكِ الشَّقَاءِ وَشَمَاتَةِ الْأَعْدَاءِ وَسُوءِ الْقَضَاءِ وَجَهْدِ الْبَلَاءِ
latin: allahumma inni a'udhu bika min darakish-shaqa'i wa shamatati'l-a'da'i wa su'il-qada'i wa jahdil-bala'
translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from falling into wretchedness, from the gloating of enemies, from bad destiny, and from severe calamity.
notes: One of the comprehensive refuge supplications taught by the Prophet ﷺ against the most painful aspects of worldly hardship.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ used to seek refuge from these four things. 'Shama'tah al-a'da'' (enemy gloating) is particularly significant — asking Allah not to let one's suffering become joy for those who wish harm. This dua covers both internal harm (wretchedness, bad decree) and social humiliation.

title: Supplication for a Child's Deep Understanding of Religion
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ فَقِّهْهُ فِي الدِّينِ وَعَلِّمْهُ التَّأْوِيلَ
latin: allahumma faqqihhhu fid-dini wa 'allimhut-ta'wil
translation: O Allah, grant him deep understanding of the religion and teach him the interpretation (of the Quran).
notes: The dua the Prophet ﷺ made for Abdullah ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him). Recited by parents and teachers for their children or students. For a female: 'Allahumma faqqihha fid-dini wa allimhat-ta'wil.'
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ placed his hand on the chest of the young Ibn Abbas and made this dua. Ibn Abbas became one of the greatest Quranic scholars in Islamic history, known as 'Tarjuman al-Quran' — a living proof that deep Islamic knowledge is a divine gift that must be sincerely asked for.

title: Supplication for Righteousness in Religion, Spaciousness in Home, and Blessing in Provision
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَصْلِحْ لِي دِينِي وَوَسِّعْ لِي فِي دَارِي وَبَارِكْ لِي فِي رِزْقِي
latin: allahumma aslih li dini wa wassi' li fi dari wa barik li fi rizqi
translation: O Allah, correct my religion for me, expand my home for me, and bless my provision for me.
notes: Recited when seeking blessing in one's religion, home, and daily provision (rizq).
benefits: These three requests represent the complete life of a believer: a sound religion protects the heart and faith; a spacious home provides comfort and a place for worship; and blessed provision sustains the body. Asking for all three simultaneously is a supplication for complete well-being.
source: HR. an-Nasa'i (Amal al-Yawm wal-Laylah), Ibn al-Sunni

title: Supplication for Relief from Physical Pain (The Izzah Version, 7x)
arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ أَعُوذُ بِعِزَّةِ اللَّهِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَجِدُ وَأُحَاذِرُ
latin: bismillah, a'udhu bi'izzatillahi wa qudratihi min sharri ma ajidu wa uhadhiru
translation: In the name of Allah. I seek refuge in the might of Allah and His power from the evil of what I feel and what I fear.
notes: Recited 7 times while placing the hand on the area of pain. This version uses 'bi'izzatillahi wa qudratihi' (by the might and power of Allah) — an authentic alternate wording to the version using 'billahi wa qudratihi.' Both are narrated in Muslim No. 2202.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever is suffering from illness — let him place his hand on the place of pain and say Bismillah three times, then seven times: I seek refuge in the might of Allah and His power from the evil of what I feel and what I fear.' This version emphasizes Allah's izzah (might and honour) as the source of healing and protection.

title: Praise of Allah as Originator of the Heavens and Earth (Surah Fatir)
arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ فَاطِرِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ
latin: alhamdulillahi fatiras-samawati wal-ard
translation: All praise be to Allah, Originator of the heavens and earth.
notes: This is the opening verse of Surah Fatir. 'Fatir' (فاطر) means the One who creates from nothing — a unique attribute of Allah distinct from ordinary creation.
benefits: Reciting and reflecting on this phrase fills the heart with gratitude and awe of Allah's creative power. This verse opens one of the Quranic chapters that glorifies Allah's majesty and uniqueness as the sole Creator.

title: Praise of Allah Who Created Darkness and Light (Surah Al-An'am)
arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَجَعَلَ الظُّلُمَاتِ وَالنُّورَ
latin: alhamdulillahilladhi khalaqas-samawati wal-arda wa ja'alaz-dhulumati wan-nur
translation: All praise be to Allah, Who created the heavens and earth, and brought into being darkness and light.
notes: The opening of Surah Al-An'am. A comprehensive praise recognising Allah's creation of the physical universe and its fundamental contrasts — light and darkness.
benefits: Reciting this verse cultivates gratitude to Allah for all of creation. It reminds us that even darkness was created deliberately as part of Allah's perfect plan, not as an accident or absence.

title: Praise of Allah Whose Dominion Spans This World and the Hereafter (Surah Saba)
arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ وَهُوَ الْحَكِيمُ الْخَبِيرُ
latin: alhamdulillahilladhi lahu ma fis-samawati wa ma fil-ardi wa lahul-hamdu fil-akhirati wa huwal-hakimul-khabir
translation: All praise be to Allah, to Whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on earth, and His is the praise in the Hereafter. He is the Wise, the All-Aware.
notes: Opening of Surah Saba. A declaration that Allah's praise is not limited to this world — it extends into the Hereafter, making it eternal and all-encompassing.
benefits: This verse is a reminder that praise belongs entirely to Allah — not only now but forever in the Hereafter. It instils deep awareness that all creation serves to glorify Him in both worlds.

title: Glorification of Allah for the Miracle of the Night Journey (Surah Al-Isra)
arabic: سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي أَسْرَى بِعَبْدِهِ لَيْلًا مِّنَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ الْأَقْصَى
latin: subhanalladhi asra bi'abdihi laylan minal-masjidil-harami ilal-masjidil-aqsa
translation: Glory be to the One Who took His servant on a night journey from the Sacred Mosque to the Farthest Mosque.
notes: The opening of Surah Al-Isra. The word 'subhana' marks this as a declaration of Allah's absolute transcendence and limitless power — typically used as a tasbih in prayer and daily dhikr.
benefits: Reciting this verse is a remembrance of Islam's greatest miracle and glorifies Allah for His boundless power. It is a reminder that no barrier — distance, time, or physical law — limits what Allah can do for His servants.

title: Prophet Nuh's Supplication for Help When Overpowered by His People
arabic: فَدَعَا رَبَّهُ أَنِّي مَغْلُوبٌ فَانتَصِرْ
latin: fada'a rabbahu anni maghlubun fantasir
translation: So he called upon his Lord: 'I have been overcome, so help me.'
notes: From Surah Al-Qamar (54:10). This is the dua of Nuh 'alayhi as-salam when his people rejected him after centuries of calling them to Allah. It is one of the shortest and most powerful cries for divine help in the Quran.
benefits: A dua to recite when one feels overwhelmed, helpless, or oppressed. Its brevity shows that a sincere cry of the heart is enough for Allah to answer. After this prayer, Allah sent the great flood — proof that He never abandons those who call on Him.

title: Prophet Nuh's Comprehensive Prayer for Forgiveness of All Believers
arabic: رَّبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِوَالِدَيَّ وَلِمَن دَخَلَ بَيْتِيَ مُؤْمِنًا وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ وَلَا تَزِدِ الظَّالِمِينَ إِلَّا تَبَارًا
latin: rabbi ighfir li wa liwalidayya wa liman dakhala bayti mu'minan wa lil-mu'minina wal-mu'minati wa la tazidiz-zalimina illa tabara
translation: My Lord! Forgive me and my parents, and whoever enters my home as a believer, and all believing men and women. And increase the wrongdoers only in destruction.
notes: From Surah Nuh (71:28). This expansive prayer encompasses personal forgiveness, forgiveness for parents, household, and the entire Ummah — making it one of the most comprehensive duas in the Quran.
benefits: This prayer teaches Muslims to always include others — especially parents and all believers — in their supplications. It is a model of selflessness in dua. Scholars recommend reciting it regularly as intercession for the entire Muslim community.

title: Prophet Nuh's Supplication Seeking Refuge from Speaking Without Knowledge
arabic: رَبِّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ أَن أَسْأَلَكَ مَا لَيْسَ لِي بِهِ عِلْمٌ وَإِلَّا تَغْفِرْ لِي وَتَرْحَمْنِي أَكُن مِّنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
latin: rabbi inni a'udhu bika an as'alaka ma laysa li bihi 'ilmun wa illa taghfir li wa tarhamni akun minal-khasirin
translation: My Lord! I seek refuge in You from asking what I have no knowledge of. And if You do not forgive me and have mercy on me, I will be among the losers.
notes: From Surah Hud (11:47). Nuh said this after Allah informed him that his son was not among the righteous. It teaches the proper adab of dua — not asking for things one has no knowledge about.
benefits: A supplication for humility in making dua. It teaches us to seek refuge from requesting things that may not be good for us, and to rely entirely on Allah's mercy and forgiveness rather than our own understanding.

title: Prophet Ibrahim's Supplication While Raising the Foundations of the Ka'bah
arabic: رَبَّنَا تَقَبَّلْ مِنَّا ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ ۞ رَبَّنَا وَاجْعَلْنَا مُسْلِمَيْنِ لَكَ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِنَا أُمَّةً مُّسْلِمَةً لَّكَ وَأَرِنَا مَنَاسِكَنَا وَتُبْ عَلَيْنَا ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ
latin: rabbana taqabbal minna innaka antas-sami'ul-'alim. rabbana waj'alna muslimayni laka wa min dhurriyyatina ummatan muslimatan laka wa ariyamana manasikana wa tub 'alayna innaka antat-tawwabur-rahim
translation: Our Lord, accept this from us. You are truly the All-Hearing, All-Knowing. Our Lord! Make us both devoted to You, and from our descendants a community devoted to You. Show us our rites of worship, and turn to us in forgiveness. You are truly the Accepter of Repentance, the Most Merciful.
notes: From Surah Al-Baqarah (2:127-128). Ibrahim and Ismail said this while building the Ka'bah. Despite performing one of the greatest acts of worship, they still prayed for its acceptance — exemplifying profound humility.
benefits: A dua for acceptance of good deeds. The lesson is profound: Ibrahim was building the Ka'bah itself yet was not certain of acceptance. Recite this after completing any good deed to ask Allah to accept it.

title: Prophet Ibrahim's Prayer for the Safety and Security of Mecca
arabic: رَبِّ اجْعَلْ هَٰذَا الْبَلَدَ آمِنًا وَاجْنُبْنِي وَبَنِيَّ أَن نَّعْبُدَ الْأَصْنَامَ
latin: rabbij'al hadhal-balada aminan wajnubni wa baniyya an na'budal-asnam
translation: My Lord! Make this city secure, and keep me and my children away from worshipping idols.
notes: From Surah Ibrahim (14:35-36). One of the founding supplications for the blessed city of Mecca, showing Ibrahim's concern for both his city's physical safety and his family's spiritual protection from shirk.
benefits: A dua for protection of one's community and family from idolatry and corruption. Scholars recommend it for parents wishing to safeguard their children's faith, and for those living in morally challenging environments.

title: Prophet Ibrahim's Prayer for Wisdom, Righteousness, and Inheriting Paradise
arabic: رَبِّ هَبْ لِي حُكْمًا وَأَلْحِقْنِي بِالصَّالِحِينَ ۞ وَاجْعَل لِّي لِسَانَ صِدْقٍ فِي الْآخِرِينَ ۞ وَاجْعَلْنِي مِن وَرَثَةِ جَنَّةِ النَّعِيمِ
latin: rabbi hab li hukman wa alhiqni bis-salihin. waj'al li lisana sidqin fil-akhirin. waj'alni min warathati jannatin-na'im
translation: My Lord, grant me wisdom and join me with the righteous. And grant me an honourable mention among later generations. And make me one of the inheritors of the Garden of Bliss.
notes: From Surah Ash-Shu'ara (26:83-85). Three connected supplications of Ibrahim covering spiritual wisdom, a lasting righteous legacy, and the ultimate destination of Paradise.
benefits: A comprehensive dua encompassing three key desires of a believer: wisdom, a righteous legacy that outlasts one's lifetime, and admission to Paradise. Scholars recommend reciting it regularly as it covers all dimensions of spiritual aspiration.

title: Prophet Lut's Prayer for Help Against the Corrupt People
arabic: رَبِّ انصُرْنِي عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْمُفْسِدِينَ
latin: rabbi unsurni 'alal-qawmil-mufsidin
translation: My Lord! Help me against the people who spread corruption.
notes: From Surah Al-Ankabut (29:30). Lut's brief supplication when facing the corrupt people who rejected his message and tried to harm his guests. A model of directness in calling on Allah.
benefits: A dua to recite when surrounded by corruption, injustice, or moral decay. Short enough to say in any moment of distress, yet powerful in directly asking for Allah's divine support against those who spread fasad.

title: Prophet Yaqub's Declaration: 'I Complain of My Grief Only to Allah'
arabic: إِنَّمَا أَشْكُو بَثِّي وَحُزْنِي إِلَى اللَّهِ وَأَعْلَمُ مِنَ اللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
latin: innama ashku bathhi wa huzni ilallahi wa a'lamu minallahi ma la ta'lamun
translation: I complain of my anguish and grief only to Allah, and I know from Allah what you do not know.
notes: From Surah Yusuf (12:86). Said by Prophet Yaqub when questioned about his prolonged grief over the loss of Yusuf. Not a conventional request — it is a statement of tawakkul and exclusive reliance on Allah.
benefits: A statement to recite in moments of intense personal grief or loss when others cannot understand your pain. It redirects the believer away from complaining to people toward Allah alone — the Only One who truly knows and cares about our private suffering.

title: Prophet Yusuf's Prayer: 'Let Me Die as a Muslim and Join the Righteous'
arabic: رَبِّ قَدْ آتَيْتَنِي مِنَ الْمُلْكِ وَعَلَّمْتَنِي مِن تَأْوِيلِ الْأَحَادِيثِ ۚ فَاطِرَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ أَنتَ وَلِيِّي فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ ۖ تَوَفَّنِي مُسْلِمًا وَأَلْحِقْنِي بِالصَّالِحِينَ
latin: rabbi qad ataytani minal-mulki wa 'allamtani min ta'wilil-ahadith. fatiras-samawati wal-ardi anta waliyyi fid-dunya wal-akhira. tawaffani musliman wa alhiqni bis-salihin
translation: My Lord! You have granted me authority and taught me the interpretation of dreams. O Creator of the heavens and earth! You are my Protector in this world and the next. Let me die as a Muslim and join me with the righteous.
notes: From Surah Yusuf (12:101). Spoken at the peak of his power as a king of Egypt. Despite having everything worldly, his greatest wish was to die as a Muslim and be with the righteous — a profound lesson in spiritual priorities.
benefits: A dua for a good ending (husnul khatimah). One of the most beautiful prayers in the Quran. Scholars recommend reciting it regularly to always keep the desire for a good death alive in one's heart, regardless of worldly circumstances.

title: Prophet Musa's Sincere Repentance and Plea for Forgiveness
arabic: رَبِّ إِنِّي ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِي فَاغْفِرْ لِي
latin: rabbi inni zhalamtu nafsi faghfir li
translation: My Lord! I have wronged myself, so forgive me.
notes: From Surah Al-Qasas (28:16). Said by Musa after an unintended act led to a man's death. Despite being a prophet, he immediately turned to Allah in sincere repentance — showing the proper response to any mistake.
benefits: One of the most direct repentance duas in the Quran. Short, sincere, and immediate. Scholars recommend reciting it right after any sin, mistake, or shortcoming, following the example of the prophets who never delayed seeking Allah's forgiveness.

title: Prophet Musa's Humble Expression of Need Before Allah
arabic: رَبِّ إِنِّي لِمَا أَنزَلْتَ إِلَيَّ مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَقِيرٌ
latin: rabbi inni lima anzalta ilayya min khayrin faqir
translation: My Lord! I am truly in need of whatever good You may send down to me.
notes: From Surah Al-Qasas (28:24). Said by Musa when he was alone, tired, and without provision after fleeing Egypt. Despite being a prophet, he acknowledged his complete neediness before Allah.
benefits: A beautiful expression of faqr (poverty before Allah) — one of the highest spiritual stations in Islam. Recite this daily as a reminder that all good comes from Allah and that we are always in need of His blessings, even when things seem fine.

title: Prophet Musa's Prayer for an Open Heart, Ease in Affairs, and Clear Speech
arabic: رَبِّ اشْرَحْ لِي صَدْرِي ۞ وَيَسِّرْ لِي أَمْرِي ۞ وَاحْلُلْ عُقْدَةً مِّن لِّسَانِي ۞ يَفْقَهُوا قَوْلِي
latin: rabbi ishrah li sadri wa yassir li amri wahlul 'uqdatan min lisani yafqahu qawli
translation: My Lord, uplift my heart for me, and make my task easy for me, and remove the impediment from my tongue, so that they may understand my speech.
notes: From Surah Ta-Ha (20:25-28). Musa's prayer before his mission to Pharaoh — covering mental clarity, ease of burden, and effective communication. Three essential requests in one dua.
benefits: One of the most widely recited Quranic duas — especially before presentations, public speaking, teaching, and studying. It covers three essential aspects: mental openness, task facilitation, and clear communication. Scholars recommend it before khutbah, lectures, or any important task.

title: Prophet Musa's Refuge from Being Among the Ignorant
arabic: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ أَن أَكُونَ مِنَ الْجَاهِلِينَ
latin: a'udhu billahi an akuna minal-jahilin
translation: I seek refuge in Allah from being among the ignorant.
notes: From Surah Al-Baqarah (2:67). Said by Musa when the Israelites questioned whether he was joking about Allah's command to sacrifice a cow. He quickly distanced himself from any attitude of mockery or heedlessness toward Allah's commands.
benefits: A short refuge dua recited when one is tempted to act with ignorance, arrogance, or mockery toward religion. It is a reminder to approach all of Allah's commands with seriousness and reverence rather than questioning or dismissal.

title: Prophet Hud's Proclamation of Complete Trust and Reliance in Allah
arabic: إِنِّي تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اللَّهِ رَبِّي وَرَبِّكُمْ ۚ مَّا مِن دَابَّةٍ إِلَّا هُوَ آخِذٌ بِنَاصِيَتِهَا
latin: inni tawakkaltu 'alallahi rabbi wa rabbikum ma min dabbatin illa huwa akhidhun binasiyatiha
translation: I have put my trust in Allah — my Lord and your Lord. There is no living creature that He does not hold by its forelock.
notes: From Surah Hud (11:56). Said by Hud 'alayhi as-salam to his people when they threatened to harm him. A declaration showing complete tawakkul (reliance on Allah) in the face of danger.
benefits: Recite this when facing threats, adversity, or fear. It reassures the believer that Allah has absolute control over every living being — nothing can harm you except by His permission. A statement of unshakeable trust used by a prophet under mortal threat.

title: Prophet Zakariyya's Prayer: 'Do Not Leave Me Without an Heir'
arabic: رَبِّ لَا تَذَرْنِي فَرْدًا وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ الْوَارِثِينَ
latin: rabbi la tadharni fardan wa anta khayrul-warithin
translation: My Lord! Do not leave me without offspring. You are the best of inheritors.
notes: From Surah Al-Anbiya (21:89). Zakariyya's prayer for a child in his old age. The acknowledgment that Allah is 'the best of inheritors' shows his surrender — if no child comes, everything will return to Allah anyway.
benefits: A powerful dua for those seeking children, fearing loneliness in old age, or worried about the continuity of their family's legacy. Allah answered this prayer with the miraculous birth of Yahya 'alayhi as-salam.

title: Prophet Zakariyya's Prayer for Righteous Offspring (Aal-Imran Version)
arabic: رَبِّ هَبْ لِي مِن لَّدُنكَ ذُرِّيَّةً طَيِّبَةً ۖ إِنَّكَ سَمِيعُ الدُّعَاءِ
latin: rabbi hab li min ladunka dhurriyyatan tayyibatan innaka sami'ud-du'a
translation: My Lord! Grant me by Your grace righteous offspring. You are truly the Hearer of all prayers.
notes: From Surah Aal-Imran (3:38). Zakariyya's prayer was answered with the miraculous birth of Yahya (John the Baptist). He acknowledged that Allah hears every prayer, no matter how private or seemingly impossible.
benefits: A recommended dua for parents and those wishing for children. Emphasises asking for offspring who are 'tayyib' (good, pure, and righteous) — not just any children, but children who will be a source of goodness and blessing.

title: Prophet Zakariyya's Prayer for an Heir Who Will Carry on His Mission (Maryam Version)
arabic: رَبِّ هَبْ لِي مِن لَّدُنكَ وَلِيًّا ۞ يَرِثُنِي وَيَرِثُ مِنْ آلِ يَعْقُوبَ ۖ وَاجْعَلْهُ رَبِّ رَضِيًّا
latin: rabbi hab li min ladunka waliyyan yarithuni wa yarithu min ali ya'quba waj'alhu rabbi radiyya
translation: My Lord! Grant me, from Yourself, an heir who will inherit from me and from the family of Jacob. And make him, my Lord, pleasing to You.
notes: From Surah Maryam (19:5-6). The fuller version of Zakariyya's dua, emphasising his wish for an heir who would carry on his prophetic legacy and be pleasing to Allah — not merely a child for personal comfort.
benefits: A dua for those seeking righteous children who will carry on good work, scholarship, or faith-based family values. Also recited by those in leadership or knowledge who wish for worthy successors.

title: Prophet Ayyub's Cry to Allah in the Midst of Suffering
arabic: أَنِّي مَسَّنِيَ الضُّرُّ وَأَنتَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِينَ
latin: anni massaniyad-durru wa anta arhamur-rahimin
translation: Adversity has touched me, and You are the Most Merciful of the merciful.
notes: From Surah Al-Anbiya (21:83). Prophet Ayyub's supplication after years of severe illness and suffering. Remarkably, it is not phrased as a direct request — he simply states his condition and names Allah's mercy, leaving the outcome entirely to Allah.
benefits: One of the most renowned duas for illness, pain, and prolonged hardship. Its power lies in complete surrender — not demanding, just presenting your state before Allah and trusting His mercy. Scholars say this teaches the highest form of dua: stating your need and trusting Allah's wisdom.

title: Prophet Yunus's Supplication from the Belly of the Whale
arabic: لَّا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
latin: la ilaha illa anta subhanaka inni kuntu minaz-zhalimin
translation: There is no god except You. Glory be to You! I have truly been among the wrongdoers.
notes: From Surah Al-Anbiya (21:87). Made by Yunus from inside the whale at the bottom of the sea. Combines tawhid (declaration of Allah's oneness), tasbih (glorification), and humble acknowledgment of one's own wrongdoing.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'No Muslim makes du'a with these words in any matter except that Allah will answer his supplication.' (HR. at-Tirmidhi No. 3505). Among the most powerful duas known — combining the highest declarations of faith with sincere acknowledgment of one's failings.

title: Prophet Sulayman's Prayer for Forgiveness and a Kingdom None Will Match
arabic: رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَهَبْ لِي مُلْكًا لَّا يَنبَغِي لِأَحَدٍ مِّن بَعْدِي ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ الْوَهَّابُ
latin: rabbi ighfir li wa hab li mulkan la yanbaghi li ahadin min ba'di innaka antal-wahhab
translation: My Lord! Forgive me, and grant me a kingdom, the like of which will not belong to anyone after me. You are indeed the Most Generous Giver.
notes: From Surah Sad (38:35). Sulayman's prayer combines humility (seeking forgiveness) with an extraordinary request for a unique kingdom. He began with seeking forgiveness before asking for worldly blessing — the proper adab of dua.
benefits: Teaches the proper etiquette of dua — always beginning with seeking forgiveness before making any request. Allah honoured Sulayman's prayer fully, granting him dominion over humans, jinn, animals, and the wind. A reminder that Allah is al-Wahhab, the Most Generous Giver.

title: Prayer for Gratitude, Righteous Deeds, and Righteous Descendants (Al-Ahqaf)
arabic: رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِي أَن أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ وَعَلَىٰ وَالِدَيَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صَالِحًا تَرْضَاهُ وَأَصْلِحْ لِي فِي ذُرِّيَّتِي ۖ إِنِّي تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ وَإِنِّي مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ
latin: rabbi awzi'ni an ashkura ni'matakallatī an'amta 'alayya wa 'ala walidayya wa an a'mala salihan tardahu wa aslih li fi dhurriyyati inni tubtu ilayka wa inni minal-muslimin
translation: My Lord! Inspire me to always be thankful for Your favours which You blessed me and my parents with, and to do good deeds that please You. And instil righteousness in my offspring. I truly repent to You, and I truly submit to You.
notes: From Surah Al-Ahqaf (46:15). A comprehensive prayer for gratitude, righteous action, righteous children, and sincere submission. Often associated with the prayer of a believer at age 40 — the stage of wisdom and reflection on one's legacy.
benefits: One of the most complete duas in the Quran. Scholars especially recommend it for those who have reached middle age — when one sees blessings more clearly, worries about legacy, and grows more conscious of the final destination.

title: The Prophet Muhammad ﷺ's Prayer: 'My Lord, Judge with Truth'
arabic: رَبِّ احْكُم بِالْحَقِّ ۗ وَرَبُّنَا الرَّحْمَٰنُ الْمُسْتَعَانُ عَلَىٰ مَا تَصِفُونَ
latin: rabbi ahkum bil-haqq. wa rabbunar-rahmanu-l-musta'anu 'ala ma tasifun
translation: My Lord! Judge with truth. And our Lord is the Most Compassionate, Whose help is sought against what you claim.
notes: From Surah Al-Anbiya (21:112). The Prophet Muhammad ﷺ was commanded to say this when his people accused him of falsehood. A prayer for truth to prevail and for Allah's judgment in matters of dispute.
benefits: A dua to recite when falsely accused, when facing injustice, or when truth is being suppressed. It places judgment in Allah's hands — the Most Compassionate — and seeks His help against slander and false claims.

title: Dua al-Iftitah: Opening Supplication at the Start of Prayer
arabic: سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ وَبِحَمْدِكَ وَتَبَارَكَ اسْمُكَ وَتَعَالَى جَدُّكَ وَلَا إِلَهَ غَيْرُكَ
latin: subhanakallahumma wa bihamdika wa tabarakasmuka wa ta'ala jadduka wa la ilaha ghayruk
translation: Glory and praise be to You, O Allah. Blessed is Your name, Exalted is Your majesty. There is no god but You.
notes: Recited silently after the opening takbir (Allahu Akbar) and before Surah Al-Fatiha in prayer. The most commonly used iftitah supplication, narrated from Umar ibn al-Khattab radiallahu 'anhu.
benefits: This opening dua sets the right spiritual tone for the entire prayer. By beginning with tasbih, hamd, recognition of Allah's blessedness, exaltation of His majesty, and affirmation of tawhid, the worshipper enters the state of full presence before Allah.
source: HR. Abu Dawud No. 775, at-Tirmidhi No. 243, an-Nasa'i

title: Prophet Sulayman's Prayer of Gratitude Upon Hearing the Ant's Warning
arabic: رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِي أَن أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ وَعَلَى وَالِدَيَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صَالِحًا تَرْضَاهُ وَأَدْخِلْنِي بِرَحْمَتِكَ فِي عِبَادِكَ الصَّالِحِينَ
latin: rabbi awzi'ni an ashkura ni'matakallatī an'amta 'alayya wa 'ala walidayya wa an a'mala salihan tardahu wa adkhilni birahmatika fi 'ibadika-salihin
translation: My Lord! Inspire me to always be thankful for Your favours which You blessed me and my parents with, and to do good deeds that please You. And admit me, by Your mercy, among Your righteous servants.
notes: From Surah An-Naml (27:19). Said by Sulayman upon hearing an ant warn its fellow ants before his army passed. This version differs from QS. Al-Ahqaf: 15 — it ends with 'wa adkhilni birahmatika fi ibadika-salihin' (admit me among Your righteous servants), a concise ending asking to be gathered with the pious.
benefits: A dua combining gratitude, righteous action, and a prayer to be included among Allah's righteous servants. The ending makes it especially powerful as a daily aspiration to be in the company of the pious both in this world and the Hereafter. Distinct from the Al-Ahqaf version which focuses on descendants and repentance.

title: Prophet Isa's Prayer for a Table from Heaven as Provision and a Divine Sign
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّنَا أَنزِلْ عَلَيْنَا مَائِدَةً مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ تَكُونُ لَنَا عِيدًا لِّأَوَّلِنَا وَآخِرِنَا وَآيَةً مِّنكَ وَارْزُقْنَا وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ
latin: allahumma rabbana anzil alayna ma'idatan minas-sama'i takunu lana 'eidan li-awwalina wa akhirina wa ayatan minka warzuqna wa anta khayru-r-raziqin
translation: O Allah, our Lord! Send down to us from heaven a table spread with food, as a festival for the first and the last of us, and as a sign from You. Provide for us — You are the Best of providers.
notes: From Surah Al-Ma'idah (5:114). Made by Isa 'alayhi as-salam when his disciples requested a miraculous table from heaven. Allah granted this prayer. The word 'eidan' (عِيدًا) means a celebration that returns — indicating a recurring blessed gift.
benefits: A dua asking for provision, blessing, and a sign of Allah's favour. It combines asking for sustenance with acknowledgment of Allah as ar-Razzaq (the Provider).
