Recited as a reminder that all provision comes from Allah and as a prayer for blessings that benefit both present and future generations.

title: Declaration of Complete Reliance on Allah When Facing a Threat
arabic: حَسْبُنَا اللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ
latin: hasbunallahu wa ni'mal-wakil
translation: Allah is sufficient for us, and He is the best Disposer of affairs.
notes: From Surah Aal-Imran (3:173). Said by Prophet Ibrahim when thrown into the fire, and by the Prophet ﷺ and his Companions when told that enemies had gathered against them. One of the most powerful Quranic declarations in Islamic tradition as a response to fear, threat, or overwhelming situations.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said Ibrahim declared this when cast into the fire — and Allah made the fire cool and safe for him. The Prophet ﷺ and his Companions also said it when facing threats after Badr. A complete declaration of tawakkul: Allah is enough, and He is the perfect Wakeel (the One who manages all affairs on your behalf).

title: Supplication Upon Sighting the New Moon (Hilal)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَهِلَّهُ عَلَيْنَا بِالْيُمْنِ وَالْإِيمَانِ وَالسَّلَامَةِ وَالْإِسْلَامِ رَبِّي وَرَبُّكَ اللَّهُ
latin: allahumma ahillahu alayna bil-yumni wal-imani was-salamati wal-islam. rabbi wa rabbukallah
translation: O Allah, let this crescent moon appear over us with blessing, faith, safety, and Islam. My Lord and your Lord is Allah.
notes: Said upon first sighting the new moon (hilal) of any Islamic month. The final phrase 'rabbi wa rabbukallah' is addressed to the moon — affirming it is a creation of Allah, not a deity. Recorded in Sunan at-Tirmidhi from the Prophet ﷺ.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ would say this upon seeing every new crescent moon. It begins each lunar month with a supplication for blessing, faith, safety, and Islam — setting a spiritual tone for the entire month ahead. Especially recommended at the sighting of Ramadan's crescent moon.

title: Supplication for Entering the Marketplace (Souk)
arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ وَهُوَ حَيٌّ لَا يَمُوتُ بِيَدِهِ الْخَيْرُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
latin: la ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamdu yuhyi wa yumitu wa huwa hayyun la yamut, biyadihil-khayru wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadir
translation: There is no god but Allah, alone, with no partner. His is the dominion and His is all praise. He gives life and causes death, and He is Ever-Living and will never die. All good is in His hands, and He has power over all things.
notes: Recited upon entering a marketplace or busy commercial area. The marketplace is described in hadith as one of the worst places in terms of heedlessness — making this dua a spiritual shield when entering a space filled with worldly distractions.
benefits: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever says this when entering the market, Allah will write one million good deeds for him, erase one million sins, raise him one million degrees, and build a house for him in Paradise.' (HR. at-Tirmidhi No. 3428, Ibn Majah). One of the most rewarding short duas for everyday commercial life.
source: HR. at-Tirmidhi No. 3428, Ibn Majah No. 2235

title: Talbiyah — The Hajj and Umrah Chant
arabic: لَبَّيْكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَبَّيْكَ، لَبَّيْكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ لَبَّيْكَ، إِنَّ الْحَمْدَ وَالنِّعْمَةَ لَكَ وَالْمُلْكَ، لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ
latin: labbayk allahumma labbayk, labbayk la sharika laka labbayk, innal-hamda wan-ni'mata laka wal-mulk, la sharika lak
translation: Here I am, O Allah, here I am. Here I am, You have no partner, here I am. Indeed, all praise, grace, and sovereignty belong to You. You have no partner.
notes: Recited continuously from the moment of entering Ihram until throwing the first pebble at Jamarat al-Aqabah on the Day of Eid (Day 10). For Umrah, it is recited until beginning Tawaf. Men recite it aloud; women recite it quietly.
benefits: The Talbiyah is the answer to the call of Prophet Ibrahim (AS) to come to Hajj. Every stone, tree, and piece of earth hears the pilgrim's Talbiyah and witnesses for them on the Day of Judgment. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'There is no Muslim who recites the Talbiyah except that everything to his right and left — stones, trees, and clods of earth — recites it along with him.' (at-Tirmidhi No. 828)
